It conducts **large-scale sample surveys** on subjects like **household expenditure, employment, industries, health**, etc. **Data collection for national income estimation** is its primary role, while **compilation is done by CSO**. NSSO also conducts the **Annual Survey of Industries** and develops techniques for **crop yield estimation**.
It provides a **nationwide statistical base** for **planning, policy making, and GDP computation**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: International Organizations (UN, IMF, World Bank, ASEAN, BRICS, etc.)
statementLines: The main watchdog of international trade is
questions.questionText: The main watchdog of international trade is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **GATT (General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade)** was established in **1947** to promote **free and fair trade** by reducing **tariffs and other barriers**.
It laid down **global trade rules for goods**, forming the **legal basis for modern international commerce**. Through multiple **GATT Rounds** (especially **Uruguay Round**1986**"94**), global trade regulations expanded to **agriculture, services, and intellectual property**.
In **1995, WTO (World Trade Organization)** replaced GATT as the global trade body but continued **GATT principles as its core agreements**. GATTs role as a **multilateral trade watchdog** was crucial for creating a **predictable, rule-based system**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: In which of the following products has Bangladesh emerged as the main competitor to India in the world market?
questions.questionText: In which of the following products has Bangladesh emerged as the main competitor to India in the world market?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Bangladesh** is the **second largest producer of jute** after India, with high-quality fiber grown in its **deltaic plains**.
It has a **70% share in global jute exports**, making it a **dominant player internationally**. India is the **largest consumer of jute domestically** due to **mandatory packaging laws**, reducing its export capacity.
Historically, **India, China, and Bangladesh** have been top jute producers, but Bangladesh leads in exports due to **focused production and processing**.
Its **low production costs and specialization** have made it Indias **main competitor in jute products globally**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which one of the following functions of the Reserve Bank of India is not performed by the other banks?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following functions of the Reserve Bank of India is not performed by the other banks?

options.d: Acting as banker to the government

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **Reserve Bank of India (RBI)** is the **central bank** and performs unique **monetary and fiscal functions** not shared by commercial banks.
**Acting as Banker to the Government** involves managing **government accounts, public debt, issuing new loans**, and representing India at **IMF & World Bank**.
Other banks can give **agricultural advances** or engage in **foreign exchange** and **refinancing activities**, but they cannot perform **sovereign banking roles**.
RBI handles **receipts and payments** on behalf of both **Central and State governments**. It also acts as **debt manager**, issuing **treasury bills and bonds**, and facilitates **ways and means advances** to governments.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which one of the following industries is not a raw material-localised industry?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following industries is not a raw material-localised industry?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Raw material-localised industries** are those located near raw material sources due to **weight loss during production** or **transport costs**.
**Glass industry** primarily requires **sand, soda ash, and limestone**, which are easily available in multiple locations. The final product is **bulky and fragile**, making it more economical to locate **near markets** than near raw materials.
Industries like **sugar, cement, cotton textiles** are typically raw-material oriented due to **perishability (sugarcane), weight (limestone)**, or **supply concentration (cotton)**.
Glass industry location depends on **market proximity, transport convenience, and energy availability**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: A common currency for the members of the Common Market in Europe is known as
questions.questionText: A common currency for the members of the Common Market in Europe is known as
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The **Euro ()** is the **official currency of the Eurozone**, which consists of 19 member states of the **European Union**.
It was introduced in **1999 as an electronic currency** and in **2002 as physical notes and coins**. The Euro was created to **facilitate trade, economic integration, and monetary stability** among EU member nations.
**Denmark and the UK opted out legally**, while other members are required to adopt the Euro once they meet convergence criteria. The Euro is also used by some non-EU countries and is the **second most traded currency globally** after the US dollar. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Transport (Roadways, Railways, Waterways, Ports, Airways, Pipelines, etc.)
statementLines: How many major ports are there at present in India?
questions.questionText: How many major ports are there at present in India?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. India has a coastline of **7,516.6 km** and operates **12 major ports** and over 200 minor/intermediate ports.
**Major ports are under Central Government jurisdiction** (Ministry of Ports, Shipping & Waterways), while minor ports fall under state governments.
Major ports include **Kandla, Mumbai, Jawaharlal Nehru, Mormugao, New Mangalore, Cochin, Chennai, Ennore, Visakhapatnam, Paradip, Kolkata, and Haldia**.
These ports handle around **55"60% of Indias total maritime cargo**, playing a key role in international trade. Several ports have undergone modernization and **PPP projects to enhance capacity and efficiency**.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Consider the following policy decisions of the Government:
1. Giving autonomy to the electronic media
2. Appointing an industrialist as the Chairman of the Indian Airlines
3. Hiring computer services on contract basis from the market
4. Selling equity shares of the State Bank of India in the market Of the above, which would constitute privatisation?
questions.questionText: Consider the following policy decisions of the Government: 1. Giving autonomy to the electronic media 2. Appointing an industrialist as the Chairman of the Indian Airlines 3. Hiring computer services on contract basis from the market 4. Selling equity shares of the State Bank of India in the market Of the above, which would constitute privatisation?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Privatisation** involves transferring ownership, control, or operations from **public sector to private entities**.
Statement 3 (hiring computer services) reflects **outsourcing of government functions** to the private sector. Statement 4 (selling SBI equity shares) involves **disinvestment, a core tool of privatisation**, reducing government stake.
Giving autonomy (1) is **not privatisation**, as ownership remains public. Appointing an industrialist as chairman (2) is a **management decision, not ownership transfer**.
Privatisation gained momentum in **post -**1991**reforms**, focusing on efficiency, competitiveness, and fiscal consolidation.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Consider the following objectives of the Budget proposals:
1. Encouraging the use of indigenous intermediate products
2. Reducing the incidence of indirect taxes
3. Simplifying the procedure for collecting indirect taxes
4. Integrating all indirect taxes in one coordinated tax MODVAT was introduced to achieve
questions.questionText: Consider the following objectives of the Budget proposals: 1. Encouraging the use of indigenous intermediate products 2. Reducing the incidence of indirect taxes 3. Simplifying the procedure for collecting indirect taxes 4. Integrating all indirect taxes in one coordinated tax MODVAT was introduced to achieve
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **MODVAT (Modified Value Added Tax)** was introduced in **1986** to **reduce cascading of excise duties** and promote efficient tax credit mechanisms.
It aimed to: o **Encourage indigenous intermediate goods** by allowing credit for duties paid on inputs. o **Reduce indirect tax burden** on final products by offsetting input tax.
o **Simplify excise procedures**, making compliance easier for manufacturers. MODVAT applied to a selected list of items and **later evolved into CENVAT**, and finally into the GST regime.
It represented a major **reform in Indias indirect taxation structure**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The term Fiscal Crisis in India currently refers primarily to
questions.questionText: The term Fiscal Crisis in India currently refers primarily to

options.c: Recurring deficit on current account in the government budget

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Fiscal crisis in India** refers to the situation of **recurring fiscal deficits** " when government expenditure exceeds revenue year after year.
In the late 1980s, India faced **widening fiscal deficits**, leading to a balance of payments crisis in **1991**. Types of deficit include: o **Revenue deficit** = Revenue expenditure " Revenue receipts. o **Fiscal deficit** = Total expenditure " (Revenue receipts + Non-debt capital receipts).
o **Primary deficit** = Fiscal deficit " Interest payments. Persistent deficits reflect **structural weaknesses in revenue generation** and high subsidies, defense spending, and administrative costs.
Such a situation leads to **higher borrowing, crowding out private investment, inflationary pressures, and external vulnerability**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Global Groupings and Agreements (FTA, Paris Agreement, NPT, QUAD, etc.)
statementLines: The Uruguay round of negotiations is primarily concerned with reduction in
questions.questionText: The Uruguay round of negotiations is primarily concerned with reduction in

options.a: The debt burden of developing countries
options.b: Restrictions on the operation of a flexible exchange rate system
options.c: Restrictions on trade in agriculture and services
options.d: Restrictions on fishing rights on the high seas

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **Uruguay Round (**1986**"**1994**)** was the 8th multilateral trade negotiation under **GATT**, involving 123 countries.
It **expanded global trade rules** to cover agriculture, textiles, services, intellectual property, and investment " areas earlier outside GATTs scope.
A key focus was **reducing trade barriers in agriculture and services**, which faced heavy subsidies, tariffs, and non-tariff restrictions in developed countries.
The negotiations led to the **establishment of the WTO in 1995**, transforming global trade governance. The round emphasized **tariff reduction, market access, trade liberalization**, and fair competition, especially benefiting developing countries seeking agricultural market entry.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: History
statementLines: The Indian caste system in its classical form is characterised by
questions.questionText: The Indian caste system in its classical form is characterised by
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **classical caste system in India** is marked by **inter-caste occupational competition**, where different jatis (sub-castes) traditionally competed for economic functions.
Each caste historically had **specific occupations** (e.g., Brahmins " priests, Kshatriyas " warriors, Vaishyas " traders, Shudras " laborers).
Over time, **competition for resources, patronage, and power** among castes intensified, especially during colonial and post-colonial periods.
**Urbanization, industrialization, and education** led to mobility and occupational overlap, breaking rigid caste roles but increasing inter-caste competition in modern sectors.
This competition is also visible in **political mobilization and reservation policies**, where caste identities influence access to opportunities.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: India experienced a sharp decline in foodgrain production when compared to the previous year, during
questions.questionText: India experienced a sharp decline in foodgrain production when compared to the previous year, during
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The years **1983"84,**1985**"86, and**1986**"87 witnessed sharp dips in foodgrain production** due to monsoon failures, drought-like conditions, and uneven rainfall.
Indias **agriculture is heavily monsoon dependent**, and fluctuations in rainfall directly affect kharif crop yields, especially rice and coarse cereals.
These declines impacted **food security, buffer stocks, and agricultural growth rates**, causing rural distress in several states. Government measures during this period included **increased imports, public distribution strengthening, and drought relief programs**.
The period highlighted the **vulnerability of Indian agriculture** despite Green Revolution advances. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Current Affairs Focus (Summits, Reports, Conflicts, Visits, etc.)
statementLines: The country which has the largest foreign debt at present is
questions.questionText: The country which has the largest foreign debt at present is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Mexico had the largest foreign debt** among developing countries during the 1980s, leading to the **Latin American Debt Crisis**.
**Foreign debt** refers to outstanding loans owed by one country to foreign creditors, including governments, international institutions (e.g., IMF, World Bank), and private lenders.
Mexico borrowed heavily during the **1970s oil boom**, but falling oil prices and rising global interest rates in the 1980s made debt servicing unsustainable.
In **1982**, **Mexico announced it could not meet its repayment obligations**, triggering a global financial panic and IMF-led restructuring.
Mexicos debt crisis became a **catalyst for debt rescheduling programs** and policy reforms in other developing countries. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Consider the following commodities:
1. Tea
2. Cotton cloth
3. Cereals
4. Edible oils
5. Sugarcane Among these, whose per capita availability has remained more or less static between 1980-81 and 1987-88 in India?
questions.questionText: Consider the following commodities: 1. Tea 2. Cotton cloth 3. Cereals 4. Edible oils 5. Sugarcane Among these, whose per capita availability has remained more or less static between 1980-81 and 1987-88 in India?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The per capita availability of **Tea, Edible oils, and Sugarcane showed no significant change** between **1980**"81 and **1987**"88, indicating stagnant production relative to population growth.
For **tea**, domestic consumption matched production, leaving little room for per capita growth. **Edible oils** faced production bottlenecks, and India relied heavily on imports during the 1980s to meet rising demand.
**Sugarcane**, while increasing in total output, was mostly used for industrial sugar production, leaving per capita availability static. In contrast, **cereals and cotton cloth saw improvements** in per capita availability due to Green Revolution gains and textile expansion.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: At present, Indias share of the world exports of tea is
questions.questionText: At present, Indias share of the world exports of tea is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **India contributes around 12"13%** of global tea exports, placing it among the top five tea exporters in the world.
The three main tea-growing regions " **Assam, Darjeeling, and Nilgiris** " produce distinctive varieties with global demand. India accounts for **~31% of world tea production**, but a large part is consumed domestically, leaving a moderate share for export.
Since **1947**, production has grown 250%, but land expansion has been only 40%, indicating **intensive cultivation and yield improvements**.
Indias **labour-intensive tea industry employs 1.1 million workers**, contributing significantly to foreign exchange earnings. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Which of the following is a cash crop?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is a cash crop?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Groundnut is classified as a cash crop**, grown primarily for commercial sale rather than subsistence consumption.
**Cash crops** include those cultivated for industrial use or export, e.g., cotton, jute, sugarcane, tobacco, coffee, tea, and **oilseeds like groundnut**.
Groundnut is mainly cultivated in **Tamil Nadu, Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh, and Maharashtra**, needing warm climate and well-drained soil. It serves as a **major raw material for the edible oil industry**, contributing significantly to Indias agricultural export earnings.
**Subsistence crops, like wheat or rice**, are grown for household consumption, while groundnut is grown for market demand and profit. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.microTopic: Allied Sectors (Animal Husbandry, Horticulture, Sericulture, Apiculture, Aquaculture)
statementLines: Stock-farming refers to
questions.questionText: Stock-farming refers to

options.a: Accent on agricultural production to build a buffer stock
options.c: Farming concentrating on the rearing of cattles and sheep

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Stock farming means livestock farming** " the practice of raising animals such as cattle, sheep, goats, pigs, and poultry for food, wool, dairy, leather, and other products.
It focuses more on **animal husbandry than crop cultivation**, often involving pastoral lands or mixed farms. In Europe and parts of India, stock farming is done in areas **unsuitable for intensive crop farming**, making animal rearing economically viable.
It provides farmers with **continuous income and food security** (milk, meat, wool, etc.) throughout the year. Recent years have seen scientific advances in **veterinary care, breeding, and feeding practices** to increase productivity.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Global Groupings and Agreements (FTA, Paris Agreement, NPT, QUAD, etc.)
statementLines: Super301 envisages retaliatory action by the United States of Americangainst the countries it believes to be
questions.questionText: Super301 envisages retaliatory action by the United States of Americangainst the countries it believes to be

options.b: Involved in the production and distribution of narcotics.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Super 301** refers to a provision under **Section 301 of the U.S. Trade Act of 1974**, empowering the U.S. to take retaliatory trade action against foreign countries.
It targets nations that violate international trade agreements, adopt unjustifiable or discriminatory trade practices, or **block fair trade with the U.S.** The U.S. President can take all appropriate action, including **imposing tariffs, quotas, or withdrawing trade concessions**.
The measure was used aggressively in the late 1980s and early 1990s, especially against **Japan, India, and Brazil** in disputes over market access and trade barriers.
Its primary objective was to **protect U.S. commercial interests** by forcing trade liberalization abroad. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Who among the following is a famous economist?
questions.questionText: Who among the following is a famous economist?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Gunnar Myrdal (1898"**1987**)** was a Swedish economist, sociologist, and politician known for his pioneering work in economic development and institutional analysis.
He won the **Nobel Prize in Economic Sciences in 1974**, jointly with Friedrich Hayek, for their analysis of monetary theory and the interdependence of economic and social phenomena.
His famous work **Asian Drama: An Inquiry into the Poverty of Nations (**1968**)** examined the developmental problems of South Asia, especially India, in depth.
He was a **strong critic of Western development models** and emphasised the role of institutions, social systems, and politics in economic growth.
His theory of **Cumulative Causation** explained how economic inequalities tend to perpetuate themselves. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: HUDCO aims at providing
questions.questionText: HUDCO aims at providing

options.b: Loan finance for housing and urban development

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. HUDCO stands for **Housing and Urban Development Corporation**, established in **1970** as a public sector enterprise.
It operates under the **Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs** and is fully government-owned. Its primary aim is to **finance housing and urban infrastructure projects**, promote planned urbanisation, and develop satellite towns.
Key functions include: o Financing construction of **affordable housing** for economically weaker sections and low-income groups. o Supporting **urban infrastructure** like roads, sewerage, drainage, sanitation, and water supply. o Subscribing to **bonds and debentures of state housing boards** and development authorities.
o Providing **technical & consultancy services** for urban planning and infrastructure. HUDCO plays a key role in **urban development schemes and low-cost housing programmes** initiated by both Central and State governments.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which sector of development has the highest percentage of the total outlay in the public sector?
questions.questionText: Which sector of development has the highest percentage of the total outlay in the public sector?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. In India, the **defence sector has consistently received the highest share** of public sector outlay, reflecting its strategic importance.
Defence is entirely under government ownership " **private participation was restricted** until the 2000s, making it a core public sector domain.
High expenditure goes into **modernisation of armed forces, procurement of arms and equipment**, salaries, pensions, infrastructure, and R&D.
Defence allocation is considered **non-plan expenditure** but occupies a large share of total budget, surpassing agriculture or industry. In the late 1980s, India was modernising its forces (e.g., INS Viraat induction, MiG aircraft procurement), leading to **large defence capital outlays**.
Sectors like agriculture and energy receive significant shares but **defence remains dominant in terms of total public sector outlay** due to security imperatives.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Who among the following is most benefitted from inflation?
questions.questionText: Who among the following is most benefitted from inflation?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. During inflation, the **value of money decreases over time**. **Debtors benefit** because they repay loans in money that is worth less than when they borrowed it.
For example, if a debtor takes a 1,00,000 loan when inflation is 3% and repays after a year when inflation is 10%, the **real value of repayment is lower**.
**Creditors lose** because the money they receive has less purchasing power. **Fixed income groups**, like pensioners or savings account holders, also lose during inflation as their **income doesnt rise with prices**.
This is why inflation tends to **redistribute wealth from creditors to debtors**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: A suitable site of a cement plant requires proximity to
questions.questionText: A suitable site of a cement plant requires proximity to
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Limestone is the primary raw material** for cement manufacture, making up about **60"65%** of the mix.
**Gypsum is used as a setting retarder** in cement, essential for controlling the hardening time. Cement plants are therefore **located near limestone and gypsum deposits** to reduce transportation costs.
**Coal is needed as fuel**, but it can be transported economically, unlike limestone which is needed in large volumes. **Proximity to raw materials ensures cost efficiency**, steady supply, and reliable production.
Example: **Chhattisgarh, Andhra Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh** are major cement-producing regions due to limestone abundance. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The Interest Rate Policy is a component of
questions.questionText: The Interest Rate Policy is a component of
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Interest Rate Policy is a tool used in monetary policy**, which is managed by the **Reserve Bank of India (RBI)**.
It involves setting **repo rate, reverse repo, bank rate, CRR**, etc., to control money supply, credit availability, and inflation. By **increasing interest rates, RBI can curb inflation** by making borrowing more expensive; **lowering rates stimulates economic activity**.
Interest rate changes also affect **investment, exchange rates, and capital flows**. **Fiscal policy**, in contrast, deals with government revenue and expenditure, not interest rates.
Interest rate policy is thus a **quantitative tool of monetary control**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: History
statementLines: The first Trade Union in India was
questions.questionText: The first Trade Union in India was
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **Madras Labour Union (MLU)**, founded in **1918 by B.P. Wadia**, is regarded as **Indias first registered trade union**.
It emerged in response to poor working conditions and exploitation in **Buckingham and Carnatic Mills in Madras**. The union organized workers, negotiated with employers, and **laid the foundation for trade unionism in India**.
The **All India Trade Union Congress (AITUC)** was formed later in **1920** as a federation of several trade unions. MLU's formation was part of the larger **labour awakening in India** during colonial times, influenced by both nationalist movements and global labour trends.
The union played a pivotal role in spreading the idea of **collective bargaining and workers rights**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Minerals and Resources (Coal, Iron, Bauxite, Lead, Zinc, Pyrites, etc.)
statementLines: Which of the following iron & steel plants is closer to coal deposits than iron ore deposits?
questions.questionText: Which of the following iron & steel plants is closer to coal deposits than iron ore deposits?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Bhilai and Bokaro** were strategically located **closer to coal reserves**, which are essential for iron and steel production.
**Bokaro Steel Plant** is located in the **Damodar Valley**, a major coal belt region, reducing transport costs for fuel. **Bhilai Steel Plant**, located in Chhattisgarh, is close to **Singrauli coal fields** and also benefits from proximity to **Korba coal fields**.
**Iron ore, though essential, is less expensive to transport compared to coal** " hence, plants were often set up near coalfields. Energy (from coal) is a key input in blast furnaces, making **coal proximity more critical than iron ore proximity**. Plants like **Jamshedpur**, on the other hand, are **closer to iron ore mines of Singhbhum**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The Ministry of Industries takes the administrative advice regarding the improvement of the performance of public enterprises from
questions.questionText: The Ministry of Industries takes the administrative advice regarding the improvement of the performance of public enterprises from
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **Bureau of Public Enterprises (BPE)** is the nodal agency for policy formulation and coordination concerning **public sector undertakings (PSUs)**.
It provides **administrative and managerial guidance** to improve efficiency, profitability, and accountability of PSUs. Functions of BPE include:
o Implementing policies on **privatisation and commercialisation**. o Advising on **capital restructuring, management, and post-transaction monitoring** of enterprises.
o Overseeing **financial discipline and performance agreements** between government and enterprises. o Preparing enterprises for privatisation and advising **NCP (National Council on Privatisation)**.
BPE serves as the **secretariat to the NCP**, ensuring smooth coordination between ministries and enterprises. Other bodies like MRTP and Planning Commission have different roles " MRTP deals with monopolies/trade practices, and **Planning Commission with overall planning**, not day-to-day enterprise performance.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The structural changes in exports means
questions.questionText: The structural changes in exports means

options.a: Change in the commodities of export.
options.b: Increasing exports along with the imports.
options.d: Change in the commodities of imports

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Structural change in exports** refers to a **shift in the composition of goods or services** that a country exports over time.
For example, if a country moves from exporting raw materials (like cotton, iron ore) to **manufactured or value-added products** (like textiles, steel), it indicates structural transformation.
It reflects changes in **industrial capacity, technological advancement, and economic diversification**. In Indias case, the post-Independence period witnessed a gradual **shift from primary goods to manufactured and service exports**.
Structural change is **different from mere volume increase** " it focuses on *what* is being exported rather than *how much*. This shift also shows **economic maturity**, as more advanced economies export processed or high-tech goods. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Which of the following agricultural holdings have a largest percentage in India?
questions.questionText: Which of the following agricultural holdings have a largest percentage in India?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Marginal holdings are defined as less than 1 hectare** of land, and they form the **largest share of landholdings** in India.
As per Agricultural Census, **marginal farmers account for around 65"70%** of total operational holdings. This reflects the **fragmented landholding structure** of Indian agriculture.
Historical factors like **inheritance laws, population pressure**, and lack of land reforms led to division into small plots. Marginal farmers often face issues like **low productivity, limited access to credit**, and lack of economies of scale. **Small holdings (1"2 ha)** form the second largest group, while medium and large holdings are minimal.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Real owners of the company are
questions.questionText: Real owners of the company are
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Equity shareholders are the actual owners** of a company as they hold ownership interest. They have **voting rights**, allowing them to participate in decision-making, elect directors, and influence company policy.
They receive **dividends based on profits** and have residual claim on assets if the company is liquidated after all obligations. **Debenture holders and bondholders are creditors**, not owners " they receive fixed interest and repayment of principal, but no ownership or control.
**Directors are responsible for managing** the company on behalf of shareholders, not owners themselves. The concept of **limited liability** applies to shareholders, meaning their risk is limited to their investment amount.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which of the following is the most industrialised country?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is the most industrialised country?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Singapore is known for its highly developed industrial economy**, making it the most industrialised among the given options.
It has a **strategic location** along major shipping routes, making it a **global trade and logistics hub**. The country has focused on **high-value industries** like electronics, petrochemicals, pharmaceuticals, and precision engineering.
With stable governance, advanced infrastructure, and **liberal trade policies**, Singapore attracted **massive FDI**, boosting its industrial growth.
By the 1980s, Singapore had already transformed from a port-based economy to a **manufacturing and financial powerhouse** in Asia. Its **per capita income and industrial output** far exceed those of Malaysia, Nepal, or Bangladesh. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Recently India suffered unfavourable balance of trade due to
questions.questionText: Recently India suffered unfavourable balance of trade due to
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Indias import bill rose sharply due to the **rising international oil prices and higher mineral imports**, widening the trade deficit.
**Petroleum crude and mineral fuels** have historically been the largest share of Indias imports, making the trade balance vulnerable to price shocks.
The 1970s"80s oil shocks led to sustained **CAD (Current Account Deficit)** issues for India. **High dependence on imported crude oil** (around 70"80%) made Indias trade deficit structurally unfavourable.
**Minerals such as fertilizers, metallic ores**, and certain industrial inputs were also imported in large quantities due to insufficient domestic availability.
During this period, Indias **export base remained narrow**, worsening the BoT (Balance of Trade) position further. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: History
statementLines: The beginning of urbanisation of the Ganges from the 6th century was mainly attributed to
questions.questionText: The beginning of urbanisation of the Ganges from the 6th century was mainly attributed to
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The Gangetic plains are among the most fertile regions in the world due to the **alluvial soil** deposited by rivers like the Ganga, Yamuna, and their tributaries.
This **fertility supported surplus agricultural production**, which is a precondition for urbanisation " **surplus allows people to engage in non-agricultural professions**.
6th century BCE saw the rise of **Mahajanapadas**, which were largely concentrated in the Ganga plains because of this fertile environment. The surplus grain enabled **trade, craft specialisation, and administrative centres**, leading to the emergence of towns and cities like Vaishali, Rajagriha, and Pataliputra.
Improved transportation through **navigable rivers** further facilitated the movement of goods and people, accelerating urbanisation. Archaeological excavations show a significant **rise in urban settlements and material culture** in this period. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Even when our foodgrain production has increased from 50 to 150 million tonnes during the last three decades, the progress has been quite unsatisfactory on agricultural development front because
questions.questionText: Even when our foodgrain production has increased from 50 to 150 million tonnes during the last three decades, the progress has been quite unsatisfactory on agricultural development front because

options.a: Only the production of a few items has gone up largely.
options.b: Only the production of pulses and edible oil seeds has recorded large increase.
options.c: Nutritional diet and 16 oz norm is still not available to our growing number of people having low purchasing power.
options.d: This has been achieved on the basis of huge quantities of imported inputs which cannot be sustained.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The increase in foodgrain production was largely due to **Green Revolution crops like wheat and rice**.
**Pulses, coarse cereals, oilseeds** saw limited productivity growth. **Regional imbalances persisted** " growth was concentrated in Punjab, Haryana, western UP.
Hence overall agricultural development remained **narrow-based and skewed**. Hence option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Which one of the following commodities has the highest production in India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following commodities has the highest production in India?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Rice is Indias largest produced crop** in terms of volume and area. It is a **staple food for majority of Indians**, grown in diverse agro-climatic zones.
India ranks among the **top producers and exporters of rice globally**. **Wheat ranks second**; tobacco and gram are comparatively much lower.
Hence option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Match the columns:
1. Pottery -
4. Mirzapur
2. Bangles -
1. Firozabad
3. Wooden carvings -
2. Trivandrum
4. Silk -
3. Bhagalpur
Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.questionText: Match the columns: 1. Pottery - 4. Mirzapur 2. Bangles - 1. Firozabad 3. Wooden carvings - 2. Trivandrum 4. Silk - 3. Bhagalpur Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Mirzapur** " famous for **pottery and clay products**. **Firozabad** " renowned for **glass bangles**.
**Trivandrum** " known for **traditional wood carving and handicrafts**. **Bhagalpur** " famous for its **silk industry, especially tussar silk**.
Hence option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Regional Rural Banks are designed to work in
which one of the following ideals?
questions.questionText: Regional Rural Banks are designed to work in which one of the following ideals?

options.a: Work on basics of Commercial Banks
options.c: Keep lending rates lower than cooperative institutions
options.d: Work on innovative and adaptive ideals

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **RRBs were set up (**1975**onwards)** to provide **credit at concessional rates** in rural areas.
Their interest rates were kept **lower than cooperatives and commercial banks**. They focused on **small and marginal farmers, rural artisans, and labourers**.
**RRBs function under NABARD supervision**. Hence option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: History
questions.microTopic: Advent of Europeans, British Expansion, Anglo Wars
statementLines: Assertion (A): In the 19th century, India became a victim of colonialism.
Reason (R): Industrial Revolution resulted in the need for more and more markets.
questions.questionText: Assertion (A): In the 19th century, India became a victim of colonialism. Reason (R): Industrial Revolution resulted in the need for more and more markets.

options.a: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
options.b: Both A and R are true but R is NOT a correct explanation of A
options.c: A is true but R is false
options.d: A is false but R is true

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **India became a victim of British colonialism** during the 19th century. The **Industrial Revolution in Britain** created a **demand for raw materials and markets**. India was turned into a **supplier of raw materials** and a **market for British goods**.
This is a **direct explanation of colonial exploitation**. Hence option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: History
statementLines: Which of the following statements are true of the Congress Socialist Party?
1. It was a forum for various kinds of Socialists in India.
2. It was a rival organisation of Indian National Congress.
3. It championed the cause of workers and peasants.
Choose the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements are true of the Congress Socialist Party? 1. It was a forum for various kinds of Socialists in India. 2. It was a rival organisation of Indian National Congress. 3. It championed the cause of workers and peasants. Choose the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Congress Socialist Party (CSP)** was formed in **1934** as a socialist caucus **within Congress**, not against it.
It brought together **Fabian socialists, Marxists, and Gandhian socialists**. It worked to mobilise **workers and peasants**, promote socialism and decentralisation.
Leaders like **Jayaprakash Narayan and Acharya Narendra Deva** were key figures. Hence option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Mechanisation of agriculture will lead to which of the following?
1. Higher per unit cost of operation
2. Higher per unit productivity
3. Higher dose of employment
4. Higher utilisation of resources
Select the correct answer from codes given below:
questions.questionText: Mechanisation of agriculture will lead to which of the following? 1. Higher per unit cost of operation 2. Higher per unit productivity 3. Higher dose of employment 4. Higher utilisation of resources Select the correct answer from codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Mechanisation often increases fixed costs, leading to **higher per unit operational cost** initially.
It also leads to **higher per unit productivity** due to efficiency in ploughing, sowing, harvesting. It usually **reduces employment**, not increases.
It may not automatically increase resource utilisation unless integrated with planning. Hence option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: One of the elements mentioned as the strategy for industrial development in the Seventh Five-Year Plan is to usher in sunrise industries. Sunrise industries refers to those industries which have
questions.questionText: One of the elements mentioned as the strategy for industrial development in the Seventh Five-Year Plan is to usher in sunrise industries. Sunrise industries refers to those industries which have

options.a: High growth potential and relevance to our needs

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Sunrise industries are new and emerging sectors** expected to dominate in future. They are characterised by **rapid growth, innovation, and strategic importance**. Examples: **electronics, biotechnology, information technology, renewable energy**.
**Seventh Plan** emphasised nurturing these for self-reliance and global competitiveness. Hence option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which one of the following is NOT an affiliate of the Reserve Bank of India?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT an affiliate of the Reserve Bank of India?

options.c: The Industrial Development Bank of India

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **UTI was created through a separate Parliamentary Act (**1963**)**. It is a **public financial institution**, not an affiliate of the RBI.
**Agricultural Refinance Corporation, DIC, and IDBI** were all established under the RBIs aegis initially. Hence option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following are under the purview of Industrial Development Bank of India?
1. Unit Trust of India
2. Life Insurance Corporation of India
3. Export-Import Bank
4. State Finance Corporation of India Choose the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following are under the purview of Industrial Development Bank of India? 1. Unit Trust of India 2. Life Insurance Corporation of India 3. Export-Import Bank 4. State Finance Corporation of India Choose the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **IDBI acts as a financial apex institution** coordinating various development finance agencies.
**UTI, EXIM Bank and State Finance Corporations** were linked to IDBI for financial and policy coordination. **LIC is an independent statutory corporation** and not under IDBIs purview.
IDBI provided **refinance, guarantees, and credit** to these bodies. Hence option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: A private company is faced with the problem of setting up a cement plant. Which of the factors given below should be taken into consideration for this venture?
1. Abundant electricity
2. Abundant limestone/dolomite
3. Abundant labour force
4. Abundant gas
5. Accessibility to transportation
6. Abundant coal Choose the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.questionText: A private company is faced with the problem of setting up a cement plant. Which of the factors given below should be taken into consideration for this venture? 1. Abundant electricity 2. Abundant limestone/dolomite 3. Abundant labour force 4. Abundant gas 5. Accessibility to transportation 6. Abundant coal Choose the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Limestone/dolomite** is the primary raw material for cement manufacture, so availability is crucial.
**Electricity** is essential for running crushers, kilns, and grinding units. **Transport connectivity (rail/road)** is critical for movement of bulky raw materials and finished cement.
Labour needs are relatively low due to mechanisation. Gas is not a necessary input in cement production. Hence option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Assume that the consumer price index (1970 = 100) is at present
500. This means that
questions.questionText: Assume that the consumer price index (1970 = 100) is at present 500. This means that

options.a: Prices of all goods and commodities have increased five times.
options.b: Prices of all consumer goods have increased five times.
options.c: Prices of all items in a specified list have increased by 400%.
options.d: Prices have increased and the weighted mean price increase for specified items is 400%.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **CPI 500** means prices have **increased by 400%** over base year. It measures change in **weighted average prices of a fixed basket of goods**.
It doesnt apply to all goods universally, only those in the **specified list**. Hence option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Among the sources of energy listed below which one is non-conventional in present day rural India?
questions.questionText: Among the sources of energy listed below which one is non-conventional in present day rural India?
questions.explanationMarkdown: **Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Biogas is classified as non-conventional** because its a modern renewable energy source. Fuel wood and cow-dung are traditional fuels. Hydel is conventional, grid-based. **Biogas plants provide clean energy and manure**, reducing dependence on forests. Hence option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
statementLines: Which one of the following has the lowest density of population per sq km according to the 1981 census?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following has the lowest density of population per sq km according to the 1981 census?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Arunachal Pradesh** has the **lowest population density** due to its large area and hilly terrain.
Other Northeastern states have low densities but higher than Arunachal. **Sparse settlement and difficult geography** are key reasons. Hence option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
statementLines: About 1.5 crore people are added every year to the total population of India. This means that we are adding every year a population equal to that of
questions.questionText: About 1.5 crore people are added every year to the total population of India. This means that we are adding every year a population equal to that of
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. In the 1980s, **Afghanistans population was around 2 crore**, similar to Indias annual population addition.
Bangladesh and Pakistan had much larger populations; Singapore was too small. This comparison highlights the **massive demographic pressure** India faced annually.
Hence option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: An ad valorem duty is a tax on the basis of
questions.questionText: An ad valorem duty is a tax on the basis of
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Ad valorem means according to value**. Tax is charged on the **value added, not on quantity**.
It is typically used in **VAT and excise structures**. This system ensures **proportional taxation** as prices change. Hence option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
statementLines: By sex ratio, we mean
questions.questionText: By sex ratio, we mean

options.a: The number of females in the population per 1000 male population.
options.b: The number of males in the population per 1000 female population.
options.c: The number of married females in the population per 1000 male population.
options.d: The number of married males in the population per 1000 female population.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Sex ratio = females per 1000 males**, as per Indian census usage. It reflects **gender balance** in a population.
This indicator is influenced by **mortality rates, migration patterns, and discrimination**. The **2011 Census recorded 940 females per 1000 males**, showing slight improvement from **2001**.
Hence option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which one of the following bodies finalises the Five-Year Plans proposals?
questions.questionText: Which one of the following bodies finalises the Five-Year Plans proposals?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **Planning Commission** prepared draft plans. The **Union Cabinet** approved the draft. The **National Development Council (NDC)** " comprising the Prime Minister, Union Ministers, and Chief Ministers " gave the **final approval**.
The NDC ensured **Centre"State coordination** and federal participation in plan finalisation. Hence option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The rate of exchange of Indian rupee is linked with
questions.questionText: The rate of exchange of Indian rupee is linked with
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. India shifted from a **single currency peg** to a **currency basket system** to better reflect trade patterns.
This helped reduce volatility arising from **fluctuations in any one currency**. The basket approach was introduced in the **late 1970s"80s** to **stabilise external payments**.
It includes major currencies like **USD, GBP, JPY, EUR** in weighted form. Hence option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Out of the following industries in India, the maximum number of workers are employed in
questions.questionText: Out of the following industries in India, the maximum number of workers are employed in
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **iron and steel industry** involves a large number of direct and indirect workers, including **mining, transportation, processing, and allied services**.
Large **integrated steel plants** like Bhilai, Durgapur, Rourkela and Bokaro employ thousands directly. The **backward and forward linkages** (e.g., coal, limestone, engineering goods) generate additional **indirect employment**.
In comparative industrial statistics of the period, **iron and steel employed more workers** than jute, sugar or textiles. Hence option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The Reserve Bank of India was established in
questions.questionText: The Reserve Bank of India was established in
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **RBI established on 1 April 1935** under the **RBI Act, 1934**. Head office moved from Calcutta to **Bombay (Mumbai) in 1937**.
Functions: **currency issuance, bankers bank, monetary policy, FX management, financial stability**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Assertion (A): Five-Year Plans form the basis of Indian economic planning.
Reason (R): India follows Soviet pattern of planning.
questions.questionText: Assertion (A): Five-Year Plans form the basis of Indian economic planning. Reason (R): India follows Soviet pattern of planning.

options.a: Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.b: Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.c: A is correct but R is incorrect
options.d: A is incorrect but R is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Assertion: True**"Indias development strategy (till **2017**) was **anchored in Five-Year Plans**.
**Reason: True**"India adopted a **Soviet-style indicative planning framework** (PC, plan outlays, targets), hence FYPs. The **Soviet model influence** explains the FYP basis. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Find the correct order of the inception
1. IRDP
2. Nationalisation of 14 banks
3. Cooperative Banking
4. Land reforms Codes:
questions.questionText: Find the correct order of the inception 1. IRDP 2. Nationalisation of 14 banks 3. Cooperative Banking 4. Land reforms Codes:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Land reforms** (post
- **1947** priority; zamindari abolition in **early 1950s**).
**Nationalisation of 14 banks (**1969**)**. **Cooperative banking** expanded early but as a formal broad step here is placed after **1969** per key milestones in the options intended chronology.
**IRDP launched 1978** (all-India by **1980**). The sequence intended by the question: **4 ' 2 ' 3 ' 1**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: National income of India during 1985-86 increased by
questions.questionText: National income of India during 1985-86 increased by
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Mid-80s growth improved** on the back of agriculture recovery, industrial uptick, and public investment, yielding **~5%+ NI growth**.
**5.1%** aligns with reported **national income growth estimates** for **1985**"86. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: International Organizations (UN, IMF, World Bank, ASEAN, BRICS, etc.)
statementLines: The World Bank provides loans to the underdeveloped countries at the rate of
questions.questionText: The World Bank provides loans to the underdeveloped countries at the rate of
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **IDA (World Banks concessional arm)** lends at highly **concessional/near-zero rates**; historically cited around **~2.5% equivalent terms** in the eras framing.
Intended for **low-income/underdeveloped countries** for **long-term development**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: In the Sixth Five-Year Plan, priority was given to
questions.questionText: In the Sixth Five-Year Plan, priority was given to

options.a: Strengthen the infrastructure for agriculture and industry both.
options.b: Removal of poverty and attainment of economic self reliance

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Sixth Plan (**1980**"85)** pivoted to **poverty alleviation (IRDP, NREP, RLEGP)** and **self-reliance** via import substitution and export promotion.
Marked shift to **social services investment** and **targeted rural employment**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: The National Policy on Education provides for opening of residential schools named Navodaya Vidyalayas for the talented children. How many such Vidyalayas were sanctioned in 1986-87?
questions.questionText: The National Policy on Education provides for opening of residential schools named Navodaya Vidyalayas for the talented children. How many such Vidyalayas were sanctioned in 1986-87?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **NPE 1986** initiated **Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas (JNVs)**"rural, residential, **merit-based schools**.
**81 JNVs were sanctioned in**1986**"87**, marking the first rollout across districts. Aim: **equity & excellence in rural education** with CBSE affiliation.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following countries has highest per capita income
questions.questionText: Which of the following countries has highest per capita income
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. In the **mid-1980s oil boom** context, **oil exporters like Saudi Arabia/Kuwait** had very high **per-capita incomes**; among the choices, Saudi Arabia is taken as highest in that specific comparative frame.
Reflects **hydrocarbon windfalls** vs diversified advanced economies. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: History
questions.microTopic: Advent of Europeans, British Expansion, Anglo Wars
statementLines: Which of the following industries was affected most due to partition?
questions.questionText: Which of the following industries was affected most due to partition?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Partition (**1947**)** split raw jute areas (East Bengal/East Pakistan) from **jute mills (West Bengal)**, severely disrupting supply chains.
**Cotton trade/logistics** were also impacted across **Punjab/Sindh**. This dual hit makes **cotton & jute the most affected**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The long-term fiscal policy formulated in the context of Seventh Five-Year Plan lays emphasis on
questions.questionText: The long-term fiscal policy formulated in the context of Seventh Five-Year Plan lays emphasis on

options.a: Tackling the problems of deep-seated poverty and unemployment
options.c: Capital formation by increasing money income and reducing real consumption
options.d: Making taxes easily comprehensible to the tax payers

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **LT Fiscal Policy** (mid-80s) oriented **public finances** to support **anti-poverty programmes**, employment, and basic needs during the **Seventh Plan**.
Emphasis on **NREP, RLEGP, IRDP**, rural infrastructure, and social sector outlays to alleviate **poverty & joblessness**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Currency of a European country which was not revalued at the European Economic Summit was
questions.questionText: Currency of a European country which was not revalued at the European Economic Summit was
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. During **EMS realignments**, **Danish Kroner** maintained its stance in a noted summit instance, while others faced revaluation/realignment.
Denmark often pegged closely within **EMS bands** and later **ERM II**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following is the correct ascending order of contribution to domestic savings?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is the correct ascending order of contribution to domestic savings?

options.a: Household sector, Corporate sector, Private sector, Public enterprises
options.b: Corporate sector, Household sector, Public enterprises, Government and Public enterprises
options.c: Government and Public enterprises, Public enterprises, Household sector, Corporate sector
options.d: Public enterprises, Corporate sector, Government and Public enterprises, Household sector.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Historically in India, households contribute the largest** to savings, followed by **private corporate, then public sector/government**.
The order presented in (a) is **ascending (smallest to largest)** across the items as grouped. Reflects dominance of **household financial & physical savings**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following is true?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is true?

options.a: Physical planning is more important than financial planning
options.b: Financial planning is more important than physical planning
options.c: Physical planning and financial planning are equally important
options.d: Physical planning and financial planning are complementary.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Physical planning (targets, capacity, projects) and financial planning** (resources, budgeting) are **interdependent**.
Effective plans require **coherence between physical objectives and financial feasibility**"they are **complementary**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Which of the following is the correct group of Kharif crops?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is the correct group of Kharif crops?

options.c: Jowar, Bajra, Rice, Cotton, Jute, Gram

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Kharif (monsoon) crops: Rice, Maize, Jowar/Sorghum, Bajra/Millet, Cotton, Groundnut, Jute, Soybean**.
**Rabi (winter) crops include Wheat, Barley, Gram, Mustard** (rules out (d)). (a) lists **classic kharif crops together**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Deficit Financing means
questions.questionText: Deficit Financing means

options.a: Difference of total expenditure and income by revenue from all sources.
options.b: Government spends in excess of revenues so that a budget deficit is incurred which is financed by the borrowings.
options.c: Difference in borrowing and external and internal resources.
options.d: Capital expenditure on items of public construction, public enterprises and public borrowings.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Deficit financing: when expenditure exceeds revenue**, the gap is **financed by borrowing/monetization**.
Used to support **plan outlays, smooth cycles**; excessive use risks **inflation and crowding out**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Largest allocation in the Seventh Five- Year Plan has been given to
questions.questionText: Largest allocation in the Seventh Five- Year Plan has been given to
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **The Seventh Plan (**1985**"90) stressed human resource development** and social justice, leading to **high allocations to education** (including Navodaya Vidyalayas, literacy).
Energy and agriculture remained priorities, but **education saw the largest allocation thrust in social sector terms** to underpin long-term growth.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Mixed cropping is useful when
questions.questionText: Mixed cropping is useful when

options.a: Crops with varying maturity periods are sown together.
options.b: Crops with same maturity period are sown together.
options.c: Different soils are used for the same crops.
options.d: Crops with different nutritional requirements are grown.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Mixed cropping/intercropping with staggered maturity** spreads risk, utilizes resources across seasons, and ensures **continuous ground cover**.
Different maturity reduces **simultaneous peak water/nutrient demand** and harvest-time bottlenecks. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following statements is not correct for economically underdeveloped countries?
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements is not correct for economically underdeveloped countries?

options.a: Involvement of a very little proportion of labour in primary occupations

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **In underdeveloped economies, a large share of labour** is in primary sector (agriculture & allied), **not very little**.
Such economies typically show **lower literacy, high unemployment/underemployment, high fertility & dependency ratios**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Crude oil was exported from India in 1985 because
questions.questionText: Crude oil was exported from India in 1985 because

options.b: Alternative source of energy has been developed in India
options.c: Import of petroleum products was much cheaper in comparison to the refined oil in India
options.d: Of insufficient refining capacity in India

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **India had limited domestic refining capacity** relative to crude availability and product demand patterns.
Export of some crude occurred while refined products were imported, reflecting **capacity and configuration gaps**. The era was marked by **BoP stress (second oil shock,**1979**; Iran"Iraq war disruptions)**, intensifying import dependence.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: History
statementLines: Indias contribution to Mathematics includes
1. Number system
2. Decimal system
3. Concept of zero
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.questionText: Indias contribution to Mathematics includes 1. Number system 2. Decimal system 3. Concept of zero Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Hindu"Arabic numerals and the place-value decimal system** evolved in India and spread via the Islamic world to Europe.
The **concept and symbol of zero (nya)** were formalized by **Indian mathematicians** (e.g., Brahmagupta, 7th c.). These innovations enabled **positional arithmetic, algebraic developments, and later calculus advances** globally.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following pairs is correct?
questions.questionText: Which of the following pairs is correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **RRBs (Regional Rural Banks) were created** (from **1975**) to provide **credit and services in rural areas** to small & marginal farmers, agri-labourers, artisans, micro-entrepreneurs.
**IDBI** was primarily an **apex development finance institution for industry** (long
- /medium-term), not short-term.
**NABARD** focuses on **agriculture and rural development** (refinance to cooperatives/RRBs, rural infra), not general industrial finance. **RBI is the central bank**; it does not provide long-term project finance; it lends short-term liquidity to banks and manages monetary policy.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
statementLines: Assertion: Sex ratio in India is in favour of males Reason: In females there is a high rate of mortality during maternity.
questions.questionText: Assertion: Sex ratio in India is in favour of males Reason: In females there is a high rate of mortality during maternity.

options.a: If A and R both are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.b: If A and R both are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
options.c: If A is correct and R is incorrect
options.d: If A is incorrect and R is correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Sex ratio in India is skewed towards males** " historically less than 950 females per 1000 males.
**High female mortality, especially during maternity and childbirth**, is a major factor. Other factors include **gender bias, female infanticide, and health neglect**.
**Maternal mortality rates were high** due to lack of healthcare access. **Both A and R are correct, and R explains A**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: The areas in India that receive approximately an average of more than 200 cm of rainfall annually are
questions.questionText: The areas in India that receive approximately an average of more than 200 cm of rainfall annually are
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Meghalaya, esp. Mawsynram & Cherrapunji**, receive worlds highest rainfall. **Assam, Nagaland, Arunachal Pradesh** also receive very heavy monsoonal rainfall. Due to **orographic effects** of hills intercepting monsoon winds.
Supports **dense forests, shifting cultivation**, and unique ecosystems. Other options include regions with **much less rainfall**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: The maximum cropped area of the country is in
questions.questionText: The maximum cropped area of the country is in
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Punjab has maximum cropped intensity** and area under cultivation. High **irrigation coverage, HYV adoption**, and **multiple cropping** contributed.
Known as the **Granary of India** post-Green Revolution. **Efficient land use** and intensive farming led to high cropping ratios. This dominance shaped **Indias food security policies**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: In 1984"85, the approximate percentage of energy produced from the following sources was Thermal | Hydel | Nuclear
questions.questionText: In 1984"85, the approximate percentage of energy produced from the following sources was Thermal | Hydel | Nuclear
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Thermal power dominated (63%)**, due to abundant coal-based plants. **Hydel power (34.5%)** significant in hilly regions like Himachal, Uttarakhand. **Nuclear power (2.5%)** was emerging, limited to few installations.
Reflects the **energy mix before liberalization**. **Thermal remained the backbone of electricity generation**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Rotation of crops means
questions.questionText: Rotation of crops means

options.a: Different crops are grown in succession to maintain the soil fertility
options.b: Growing two crops at the same time
options.c: Same crop is grown after some time

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Crop rotation involves planned sequence** of different crops on the same land. **Maintains soil fertility** by balancing nutrient use and replenishment. Helps in **controlling pests and weeds and reducing erosion**.
**Leguminous crops restore nitrogen**, cereals utilize it. A key **sustainable agricultural practice**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which of the following statements about Kalpakkam Atomic Power Plant is/are true?
1. It used for the first time the uranium-plutonium monocarbide fuel
2. It can generate up to 3,50,000 MW of power
3. It used the enriched uranium-plutonium oxide fuel
4. Only two other countries have the distinction of having a centre for atomic research like the one at Kalpakkam
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements about Kalpakkam Atomic Power Plant is/are true? 1. It used for the first time the uranium-plutonium monocarbide fuel 2. It can generate up to 3,50,000 MW of power 3. It used the enriched uranium-plutonium oxide fuel 4. Only two other countries have the distinction of having a centre for atomic research like the one at Kalpakkam
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **All statements are correct** regarding Kalpakkam. Its a **comprehensive nuclear complex** including reactors, reprocessing, and **fast breeder programmes**.
**First use of uranium"plutonium monocarbide fuel** for fast breeder research. It has significant power capacity and **research uniqueness**, shared by only a few countries.
Represents Indias **indigenous nuclear technology capability**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.microTopic: Allied Sectors (Animal Husbandry, Horticulture, Sericulture, Apiculture, Aquaculture)
statementLines: Operation Flood II refers to
questions.questionText: Operation Flood II refers to
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Operation Flood II (**1981**"85) expanded milk-sheds** from 18 to 136, and urban markets to 290 towns.
Strengthened **village milk cooperatives and self-reliant dairy networks**. Increased **domestic milk powder production** massively. Funded through **EEC grants and World Bank loans**. Core part of Indias **White Revolution**, making India the **largest milk producer**.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Direct taxation was introduced by the ____ committee?
questions.questionText: Direct taxation was introduced by the ____ committee?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Raja Chelliah Committee (**1991**"93)** laid the framework for **modern direct tax reforms**.
Suggested **rationalization of tax rates, widening the base, VAT**, and computerization. Led to **reduction in marginal tax rates** and **corporate tax rationalization**.
Integrated customs, excise, and service taxes, **paving way for GST**. Considered a **turning point in Indias fiscal reforms**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The important function of taxation in India is to
questions.questionText: The important function of taxation in India is to
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Primary objective is mobilizing resources** to fund **public goods and development**. Supports **infrastructure, social welfare, defence, and governance**. Secondary objectives include **redistribution and economic regulation**.
Taxation plays a central role in Indias **planned development model**. While it can help curb inflation or black money indirectly, its **main function is revenue generation**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Standard of living of a nation can be judged by
questions.questionText: Standard of living of a nation can be judged by

options.a: Increase in GNP at factor cost
options.b: Increase in GNP at cost including taxes and duties
options.c: Increase in NNP at factor cost

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Standard of living goes beyond just income indicators**. It includes **education, healthcare, employment quality, infrastructure, housing, and leisure**.
Pure economic indicators like **GNP/NNP dont fully capture distribution, access, or quality of life**. Modern measures use **HDI, MPI, or composite indices**.
So **none of the given options alone determines standard of living comprehensively**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Match the columns:
A. Varanasi -
1. Indian Rare Earths Ltd.
B. Koyali -
2. Hindustan Machine Tools Ltd.
C. Pinjore -
3. Diesel Locomotive Works
D. Alwaye -
4. Petrochemical complex
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.questionText: Match the columns: A. Varanasi - 1. Indian Rare Earths Ltd. B. Koyali - 2. Hindustan Machine Tools Ltd. C. Pinjore - 3. Diesel Locomotive Works D. Alwaye - 4. Petrochemical complex Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Varanasi " Diesel Locomotive Works (DLW)** manufactures diesel-electric locomotives. **Koyali " Petrochemical Complex**, a major refinery in Gujarat. **Pinjore " Hindustan Machine Tools Ltd.**, known for precision machinery.
**Alwaye " Indian Rare Earths Ltd.**, dealing with minerals and rare earths. This reflects Indias post-independence **industrial diversification**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: The Prevention of Food Adulteration Act came into force in
questions.questionText: The Prevention of Food Adulteration Act came into force in
questions.explanationMarkdown:
