**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Enacted in 1954** to ensure **pure and wholesome food** for consumers. Aimed to **prevent adulteration and fraud** in food products. Amended in **1964,**1976**, and 1986** to make punishments stricter and involve consumer groups.
Forms the basis of **India's food safety regulation** before FSSA **2006**. Strengthened legal powers for **inspection, testing, and prosecution**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: India earns maximum foreign exchange from the export of
questions.questionText: India earns maximum foreign exchange from the export of

options.c: Textile fabrics including cotton and man-made fibres

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Gems and jewellery sector** has historically been Indias **largest forex earner**. India contributes nearly **29% of global jewellery consumption**. Home to 300,000+ players and employs over **2.5 million people**.
India is the **worlds largest diamond cutting and polishing centre**. Strong **export promotion councils and modernization** have boosted competitiveness.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.microTopic: Allied Sectors (Animal Husbandry, Horticulture, Sericulture, Apiculture, Aquaculture)
statementLines: According to the Livestock Census of 1982 in India, the ratio of the cattle population to the buffalo population was approximately
questions.questionText: According to the Livestock Census of 1982 in India, the ratio of the cattle population to the buffalo population was approximately
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **The Livestock Census 1982** reported a **cattle to buffalo ratio of 2:3**. Reflects the **significant presence of buffaloes**, particularly in northern and western India. Buffaloes are preferred for **higher milk fat content**, making them economically valuable.
Cattle dominate in eastern and southern India, whereas **buffaloes dominate in Punjab, Haryana, UP**. This ratio influenced dairy policies and **Operation Flood strategies**.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following pairs is/are incorrect?
I. Consumer Price Index - Inflation
II. Export concessions - Fiscal policy
III. Imposition of taxes - Economic planning
IV. Income - Standard of living
V. Deficit budgeting - Inflation
Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following pairs is/are incorrect? I. Consumer Price Index - Inflation II. Export concessions - Fiscal policy III. Imposition of taxes - Economic planning IV. Income - Standard of living V. Deficit budgeting - Inflation Select the correct answer from the codes given below:

options.d: None of (I), (II) and (III) is a correct answer

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **I: CPI measures inflation** ' correct. **II: Export concessions are fiscal tools** ' correct.
**III: Taxation belongs to fiscal policy**, not planning ' **incorrect**. **IV: Income reflects living standards**, though not alone ' acceptable.
**V: Deficit budgeting doesn't inherently cause inflation**; depends on monetization & supply ' **treated incorrect here**. Therefore, **III and V are incorrect**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
statementLines: The year of Great Divide, in Indian demography, is
questions.questionText: The year of Great Divide, in Indian demography, is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **1921 marks the demographic divide**"shift from unstable to sustained population growth. **Mortality declined** due to better health and sanitation; **fertility remained high**. Growth rate **accelerated in subsequent decades**.
Before **1921**, population growth was **fluctuating due to famines and epidemics**. After **1921**, India entered a phase of **steady demographic expansion**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Yuan is the currency of
questions.questionText: Yuan is the currency of
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Yuan (CNY) is the basic unit of Renminbi, Chinas official currency**. Commonly used in **international trade and forex**.
Should not be confused with **Yen (JPY) of Japan or Rupiah (IDR) of Indonesia**. The currency plays a key role in **global trade settlements**.
Chinas economic rise increased the **Yuans global relevance**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Current Affairs Focus (Summits, Reports, Conflicts, Visits, etc.)
statementLines: The 1984 conference held in Maldives was about the
questions.questionText: The 1984 conference held in Maldives was about the

options.a: Developmental needs of the Third World countries
options.c: The withdrawal of U.S. from UNESCO

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Focused on **developmental concerns of Third World nations**. Addressed **economic disparities, trade terms, and technology transfer**.
Emphasized **South"South cooperation** within the Non-Aligned framework. Reflected the **global economic discourse of the 1980s**. Maldives hosted as part of broader **regional development efforts**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Which of the following is most suitable for the remote hilly villages of about 100 families where streams are tiny and slow?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is most suitable for the remote hilly villages of about 100 families where streams are tiny and slow?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Micro or mini hydel projects are ideal for small hill streams**. Provide **round-the-clock reliable power**, unlike solar which is day-dependent.
Require **low investment, minimal maintenance, and utilize local water flow**. Big dams are **economically unviable** for such scale. Widely used in remote **Himalayan and North-Eastern regions**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Of the total foodgrains produced in India during 1981"82, the percentage of pulses produced was approximately
questions.questionText: Of the total foodgrains produced in India during 1981"82, the percentage of pulses produced was approximately
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Pulses contributed around 12% to total foodgrain output**. Grown largely in **rainfed areas**, with lower yields compared to cereals.
Pulses are crucial for **protein security** in predominantly vegetarian diets. **Price support and focused productivity measures** were limited at the time.
Productivity improvements picked up pace in **later decades**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The number of families per block to be covered under the Integrated Rural Development Programme during 1980"85 was
questions.questionText: The number of families per block to be covered under the Integrated Rural Development Programme during 1980"85 was
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **IRDP (**1980**"85) targeted 3,000 families per block** for upliftment. Focused on **asset creation, subsidized credit, and skill support**. Implemented through **District Rural Development Agencies (DRDAs)** and banks.
Aimed to generate **self-employment and reduce rural poverty**. It became one of the **largest anti-poverty programmes of its time**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The maximum limit of investment in the small scale industrial units including machinery and equipment is
questions.questionText: The maximum limit of investment in the small scale industrial units including machinery and equipment is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. At the time, **7.5 lakh was the investment cap** in plant and machinery for SSI classification.
Ensured targeted policy benefits like **credit support, reservation, and subsidies**. Aimed to encourage **small entrepreneurship and employment generation**.
Limits were **periodically revised** to adjust for inflation and policy shifts. Defined a key part of **Indias industrial policy framework** in the 1980s.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: In India, the rise in food production between 1960"61 and 1980"81 was approximately
questions.questionText: In India, the rise in food production between 1960"61 and 1980"81 was approximately
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. Driven by the **Green Revolution**"use of **HYV seeds, chemical fertilizers, and irrigation expansion**.
**Wheat and rice** showed maximum productivity gains. Supported by **price support (MSP), procurement, credit, and technology dissemination**.
**Foodgrain output increased by over 60%**, reducing import dependence. Laid the base for **self-sufficiency in food production**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Approximately what per cent of the total petroleum consumption in India is obtained from within the country?
questions.questionText: Approximately what per cent of the total petroleum consumption in India is obtained from within the country?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Historically, about **20% of petroleum needs were met domestically**, showing import dependence.
Key producing regions include **Mumbai High, Assam"Arakan Basin**, and **Krishna-Godavari Basin**. Rapid demand growth outpaced **domestic exploration output**. This led to **strategic petroleum reserve policies** and diversification of imports.
India has steadily increased production, but **imports still dominate**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following Five-Year Plans was terminated one year before its completion?
questions.questionText: Which of the following Five-Year Plans was terminated one year before its completion?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Fifth Five-Year Plan (**1974**"79)** was terminated in **1977"78** by the **Janata Government**.
Replaced by a **Rolling Plan (**1978**"83)** to allow flexibility in annual targets. Later, after the fall of the Janata government, **Indira Gandhi restored**1978**"79 to the Fifth Plan**.
The plan initially focused on **poverty removal and self-reliance**. Its termination marked a **shift in planning approach**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Uttar Pradesh tops the list of sugarcane producing States in India. Which of the following States occupies the second position in this regard?
questions.questionText: Uttar Pradesh tops the list of sugarcane producing States in India. Which of the following States occupies the second position in this regard?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Maharashtra is the second largest sugarcane producer** after Uttar Pradesh. The state has well-developed **cooperative sugar mills**, particularly in western Maharashtra. **Assured irrigation, high sucrose content**, and favorable agro-climatic conditions ensure good yield.
Long crushing seasons and **efficient processing** strengthen its position. It plays a major role in **sugar exports** from India. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Maximum revenue is being earned from
questions.questionText: Maximum revenue is being earned from
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Corporation tax emerged as a leading revenue source** with profit growth/formalization, overtaking traditional excise/customs in buoyancy.
**Trade liberalization reduced customs shares**; excise evolved toward VAT/GST, changing incidence and pooling. Non-tax revenues (dividends, fees, spectrum, royalties) are episodic; **corporate tax offers stable buoyancy** with economic expansion.
Revenue structure reflects a **shift toward direct taxes**, improving progressivity and elasticity. Supports plan and non-plan spending with **predictable flows**.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: The foodgrains production in 1983-84 is an all-time record estimated at approximately
questions.questionText: The foodgrains production in 1983-84 is an all-time record estimated at approximately
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **1983**"84 saw **favourable monsoons, expanded irrigation, HYV diffusion**, and input availability drive a record harvest.
Strong **procurement/MSP and buffer stock policies** supported sowing decisions and market off-take. **Output diversification across paddy/wheat/coarse cereals** plus pulses lifted the aggregate. **Record near 150 MT signaled food security gains** and reduced import dependence.
Set a **higher baseline** for subsequent plan targets. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
statementLines: According to a recent UN report on the state of worlds population, the growth rate will be zero in
questions.questionText: According to a recent UN report on the state of worlds population, the growth rate will be zero in
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Zero population growth** arises when **fertility replacement** and population momentum has played out.
UN long-range projections anticipate **late-century convergence** as education, urbanization, female labor force participation, and health reduce fertility.
**Age structure dynamics** delay stabilization even after fertility falls; cohorts age through. **2095 aligns with a scenario where global growth approximates zero**; earlier dates are too soon given momentum.
Has profound implications for **dependency ratios, pensions, and labor markets**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Assertion (A): Though three decades have passed since Independence, still many people are living below poverty line in India.
Reason (R): Five-Year Plans have failed to achieve the targeted growth rate.
questions.questionText: Assertion (A): Though three decades have passed since Independence, still many people are living below poverty line in India. Reason (R): Five-Year Plans have failed to achieve the targeted growth rate.

options.a: A and R both are incorrect
options.b: A is correct but R is incorrect
options.c: A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.d: A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **A true: headcount poverty remained high** for decades, reflecting low per-capita incomes and limited job creation.
**R true: multiple Plans under-achieved growth**, curbing fiscal space, public investment, and employment absorption. **Growth shortfalls directly slowed poverty reduction**, given the elasticity of poverty to growth; distribution policy helps, but insufficient growth constrains the resource envelope.
Thus **R materially contributes to A** in the planning context, even though targeted anti-poverty design/implementation also matter. Empirically, **accelerated growth phases correlate with faster poverty decline**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Assertion (A): Our agricultural growth rate is small even after proper planning and huge investments.
Reason (R): The land reform measures are not carried up to the mark
questions.questionText: Assertion (A): Our agricultural growth rate is small even after proper planning and huge investments. Reason (R): The land reform measures are not carried up to the mark

options.a: A and R both are incorrect
options.b: A is correct but R is incorrect
options.c: A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
options.d: A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A.

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **A true**: growth has been modest/volatile due to **monsoon dependence, low irrigation coverage in places, input/credit constraints**, and market/price risks.
**R true**: **land reforms (tenancy regulation, ceilings, consolidation) were patchy**, limiting farm structure efficiency and tenant incentives.
But low growth also stems from **extension gaps, post-harvest/logistics, weak R&D diffusion, insurance failures**, and price uncertainty. Hence **R is one factor among many**; it does not fully explain A. **Multifactor diagnosis** means correct statements, weak causality as framed.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The novelty in the Sixth Plan was
questions.questionText: The novelty in the Sixth Plan was
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Shift to sharper sectoral articulation**: agriculture, energy, transport, industry, and social sectors with **monitorable targets**.
**Integrated energy policy approach (coal, power, petroleum)**, TOR-linked investments, and efficiency drives. Introduced stronger **program budgeting, rolling plans**, and **mid-term course corrections** anchored in sector outcomes.
Area planning existed earlier (e.g., command areas), while the distinct novelty was **sector focus and performance orientation**. Enhanced **resource prioritization, project pipeline quality**, and inter-sector coordination. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: In the Sixth Five-Year Plan, indicates allocations were made in descending order for the following sectors:
1. Irrigation and flood control
2. Agriculture and allied programmes
3. Village and small industries
4. Power Codes:
questions.questionText: In the Sixth Five-Year Plan, indicates allocations were made in descending order for the following sectors: 1. Irrigation and flood control 2. Agriculture and allied programmes 3. Village and small industries 4. Power Codes:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Emphasis on agriculture & allied** to revive growth, reduce food imports, and boost rural incomes after late-70s stress.
**Power prioritized** to alleviate bottlenecks in industry/agriculture (lift irrigation, processing). **Irrigation/flood control** important for yield stability and risk mitigation; funded after power.
**Village & small industries** supported employment and dispersed industrialization but attracted smaller shares. **Ordering consistent with 2 > 4 > 1 > 3**.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following pairs is incorrect?
Five Year Plan: Laid main emphasis on
questions.questionText: Which of the following pairs is incorrect? Five Year Plan: Laid main emphasis on

options.b: II: Expansion of basic and heavy industries
options.c: III: Self sufficiency in food and expansion of basic industries like steel
options.d: IV: Political growth of the country

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **I Plan prioritized agriculture/irrigation/community development** to address food/security bottlenecks.
**II Plan (Nehru"Mahalanobis) emphasized basic/heavy industry**, capital goods, and import substitution. **III Plan targeted food self-sufficiency, industry expansion**, and infrastructure; derailed by shocks but emphasis remains as stated.
**IV Plan focused on growth with stability and self-reliance**, price stability, employment"**not political growth.** Therefore, **IV pairing is incorrect** in theme characterization. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Price rise goes in favour of those who are
questions.questionText: Price rise goes in favour of those who are
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Inflation reduces the real burden of fixed nominal debts**; debtors repay in **cheaper money**.
Creditors/pensioners on fixed incomes **lose purchasing power unless indexed**; DA helps but with lags. Business gains from inflation depend on **pricing power, input contracts, inventory, and interest costs**; not uniformly positive.
Real interest rates may turn negative, further **favoring borrowers** if nominal rates lag inflation. **Debt contracts rarely auto-index**; redistribution occurs from **lenders to borrowers**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The major source of revenue for executing Five Year Plans is
questions.questionText: The major source of revenue for executing Five Year Plans is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Plan financing** relied heavily on **budgetary support sourced from indirect taxes (excise, customs, sales tax/VAT)**, especially in earlier plan eras.
**Broad base, easier administration, and immediate buoyancy** made indirect taxes the workhorse of revenue. Supplemented by **market borrowings, small savings, and external assistance**, but tax-buoyancy was centered on indirect heads.
Direct taxes gained share later; customs moderated with trade liberalization; **GST modernized the indirect tax base** subsequently. **Revenue composition** shaped public capital formation and plan outlay profiles. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: International Organizations (UN, IMF, World Bank, ASEAN, BRICS, etc.)
statementLines: World Bank loan is given to
questions.questionText: World Bank loan is given to

options.a: Make available the currency of a particular country in case of shortage
options.b: Countries for sound projects for development purposes
options.c: Improve the social and cultural conditions of a country

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **IBRD/IDA finance projects/programs** in **infrastructure, human development, public systems**, with economic viability and safeguards.
Instruments: **investment project financing, program-for-results, development policy financing**, plus technical assistance. Targets **poverty reduction, shared prosperity, institutional capacity**"not short-term FX shortages (IMFs remit). Appraisal covers **cost"benefit, fiscal sustainability, environment/social impacts, procurement and M&E**. Loans often accompanied by **knowledge transfer and policy dialogue**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Match the following:
A. HAL -
1. Coal
B. BHEL -
2. Aeronautical goods
C. SAIL -
3. Electrical goods
D. CIL -
4. Steel
Select the correct answer from the codes below:
questions.questionText: Match the following: A. HAL - 1. Coal B. BHEL - 2. Aeronautical goods C. SAIL - 3. Electrical goods D. CIL - 4. Steel Select the correct answer from the codes below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **HAL ' Aeronautical goods**: airframes, engines, avionics, MRO for defence/civil platforms.
**BHEL ' Electrical goods**: turbines, boilers, generators, transformers, GIS, and power plant EPC. **SAIL ' Steel**: integrated iron & steel producer across longs/flats, value-added steels.
**CIL ' Coal**: largest coal miner, backbone of thermal power fuel supply. **Code maps to 2 3 4 1**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Economy of Brazil is mostly dependent on
questions.questionText: Economy of Brazil is mostly dependent on
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Sugarcane complex (sugar + ethanol)** is a pillar: Brazil is a **leading producer/exporter**, shaping **trade balances** and **energy mix (flex-fuel vehicles)**.
**Ethanol reduces oil import dependence**, supports **rural employment**, and **stabilizes sugar markets** via divert-to-ethanol flexibility.
While coffee is iconic, **sugar/ethanols value/volume** and macro linkages are broader. Agro-export portfolio includes soybeans, meat, orange juice, tobacco, but **sugarcane ecosystem is central to industrial/agri policy**.
**Investment in mills**, cogeneration (bagasse), and logistics deepens its **systemic importance**. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Antyodaya scheme aims at
questions.questionText: Antyodaya scheme aims at

options.a: To uplift the poorest sections of the society
options.b: To provide drinking water to Harijans
options.c: To implement reservation policy for Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes
options.d: To allot land to marginal farmers

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Anchored in the **last person first ethos**, targeting **poorest of the poor** for **food security, livelihoods, and social protection**.
Instruments include **subsidized grain** (e.g., Antyodaya Anna Yojana), **asset/livelihood support, skill upgrading**, and service convergence.
Improves **consumption smoothing, nutrition, and human capital**, breaking intergenerational poverty. Selection via **deprivation criteria** ensures **progressive targeting** where fiscal space is limited.
Complements universal schemes by **deeper support to the most vulnerable**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: The most appropriate source of energy for cooking and lighting purposes in rural India is
questions.questionText: The most appropriate source of energy for cooking and lighting purposes in rural India is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Biogas from dung/crop residues** yields **clean cooking fuel** and can power mantle lamps; addresses both cooking and lighting at household/community scale.
Co-benefits: **slurry manure improves soil fertility**, reduced indoor air pollution, less deforestation, womens time savings. **Low O&M**, local feedstock availability, and **decentralized deployment** suit rural contexts. Solar excels at lighting, but cooking needs continuous thermal energy; **biogas fits 24-7 kitchen demand** better than intermittent PV without costly storage.
Nuclear/tidal are **centralized/large-scale** and unsuitable for direct rural household cooking/lighting. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Minerals and Resources (Coal, Iron, Bauxite, Lead, Zinc, Pyrites, etc.)
statementLines: The steel industry in India is lagging behind its objectives due to less availability of
questions.questionText: The steel industry in India is lagging behind its objectives due to less availability of
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Coking coal** (low ash, high coking properties) is **critical for blast furnace"basic oxygen furnace routes**; India has **limited reserves**, necessitating imports.
**Import dependence raises input costs**, FX exposure, supply risk, and affects cost competitiveness vs. coking-coal-rich countries. Encourages shifts to coal blending/beneficiation, PCI injection, and gas-based DRI-EAF routes where feasible. Constraints persist despite abundant iron ore, brownfield expansions, and public"private capacity additions. Long-term strategy: **energy efficiency, scrap recovery, logistics cost reduction**, R&D for BF efficiency, and **resource security**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following sectors mobilises maximum savings?
questions.questionText: Which of the following sectors mobilises maximum savings?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Commercial banks** are the **primary financial intermediaries** channeling household savings into the formal system via deposits.
Offer ubiquitous access (branch network), liquidity, safety (regulation, deposit insurance), and payments infrastructure, **attracting the widest saver base**.
Mobilize **small, frequent savings at scale** and transform into loans/investments, facilitating financial deepening. Corporates are mainly users (not aggregators) of savings; other FIs (LIC/UTI/NBFCs) mobilize significantly but **less broadly than banks**.
**Banking sectors role is pivotal** for public borrowing, credit creation, and plan financing through **SLR-linked investments**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Mixed farming means
questions.questionText: Mixed farming means

options.a: Simultaneous cultivation of a number of crops in a single field
options.c: Growing fruits and vegetables in the same field

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Mixed farming = crop + livestock system** on one farm for **risk diversification, nutrient cycling (manure)**, residue utilization (fodder), and cash-flow smoothing (daily milk/eggs).
Enhances factor productivity (land/labor/capital), creates synergies (draught power, manure composting), and **stabilizes income** under weather/price shocks.
Options (a) and (b) describe **mixed/alternate cropping**, (c) is horticultural intercropping; none includes livestock, so **no option captures the definition**.
Typical Indian mixed farms: cereals + pulses + dairy or oilseeds + small ruminants, leveraging **common property resources and crop residues**.
Promotes **circular bioeconomy at farm scale**, reducing external input dependence. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
statementLines: The rural population of India is about" percent of the total population
questions.questionText: The rural population of India is about" percent of the total population
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Roughly **two-thirds of the population resided in rural areas** during the period referenced, reflecting an **agrarian economy** and gradual urban transition.
Implies **large workforce share in agriculture/related activities**, with underemployment and seasonality challenges. Policy consequence: prioritize **rural roads, irrigation, health, schools**, drinking water, and non-farm enterprises.
High rural share shapes **demand patterns** (staples, basic consumer goods) and **delivery models** (PDS, primary health centers, cooperatives).
Urbanization remains incremental, not explosive, calling for **balanced regional development**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Minerals and Resources (Coal, Iron, Bauxite, Lead, Zinc, Pyrites, etc.)
statementLines: Mica is abundantly used in which of the following industries?
questions.questionText: Mica is abundantly used in which of the following industries?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Muscovite/Phlogopite mica** offer high dielectric strength, low dielectric loss, thermal stability, cleavage into thin sheets; **ideal for insulation**.
Applications: **capacitors, commutators, insulating washers, switchgear**, heater backings, microwave windows, motor windings. Micanite (mica paper with binders) forms complex shapes with **insulation integrity**. Secondary uses (paints, plastics, cosmetics) exploit platelet morphology for barrier properties and pearlescent effects, but **electrical remains core**.
Indias historical mica belts (e.g., **Jharkhand"Bihar, Rajasthan**) supported electro-insulation supply chains. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Stagflation refers to
questions.questionText: Stagflation refers to
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Stagnation + inflation**: weak/negative growth and **high unemployment coexisting with elevated prices**; breaks the simple Phillips curve narrative.
Typically triggered by **adverse supply shocks** (e.g., oil price spikes, droughts) that raise costs and depress output; **cost-push inflation dominates**.
Policy dilemma: tightening demand to curb inflation worsens unemployment; stimulus to reduce unemployment fuels inflation; solution leans on **supply-side reforms, energy diversification, productivity gains**.
Characterized by **inflation expectations becoming unanchored**; needs credible disinflation plus structural measures (competition, logistics, labor/product markets).
Historically associated with **1970s oil shocks**, but relevant to any constraint-induced inflation amid output slack. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: New high yielding varieties of food crops are different from the conventional in that they need
questions.questionText: New high yielding varieties of food crops are different from the conventional in that they need
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **HYVs are input-responsive**: they express yield potential only with **assured irrigation** and **balanced N-P-K**; water stress or nutrient deficits truncate their advantage.
Require **timely split applications of nitrogen** and adequate phosphorus/potassium, plus secondary/micronutrients (S, Zn, B) based on soil tests, to sustain tiller density and grain filling.
Depend on **package of practices**: irrigation scheduling, weed/pest management, seed rate/row spacing, and harvest timing; yield is a system outcome, not seeds alone.
Contrast with traditional landraces that tolerate low inputs and variable rainfall but yield less; **HYVs trade resilience for high ceiling yields** under managed conditions.
**Green Revolution success** hinged on canal/tube-well expansion, **fertilizer availability/credit**, and MSP procurement, which underwrote farmers risk-return calculus.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: What type of trade is being undertaken by India with East European countries?
questions.questionText: What type of trade is being undertaken by India with East European countries?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Bilateral trade** uses **government-to-government protocols** specifying lists of goods, quantities, and settlement modalities; reduces exposure to volatile third-country policies.
Enables **reciprocal market access** for priority items (e.g., machinery from them, pharmaceuticals/tea/textiles from India), often with **multi-year commitments** that stabilize orders and prices.
Can incorporate **clearing/compensation accounts**, currency swap/clearing rupee accounts, and **state credit lines**, easing hard-currency constraints.
Distinct from **free trade** (multilateral, low barriers), **barter** (direct goods-for-goods swap without monetary settlement), or purely **rupee trade** (a specific settlement form sometimes embedded inside bilateral deals).
Supports **strategic autonomy, technology access**, and **spare-parts assurance** even amid global disruptions; aligns with **import-substitution and diversification objectives**.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The rate of inflation in India in 1981"82 went down to the extent of approximately
questions.questionText: The rate of inflation in India in 1981"82 went down to the extent of approximately
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. In **1981"82, Indias inflation rate dropped to around 10%**, after experiencing higher levels in the late 1970s.
Reasons for decline: o **Improved agricultural output** due to good monsoons. o **Tighter monetary policy by RBI**. o **Moderation in global oil prices** compared to the **1979** crisis.
This period saw **stabilisation efforts to control price rise** through fiscal discipline and supply-side measures. Hence,

option b is correct

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Transport (Roadways, Railways, Waterways, Ports, Airways, Pipelines, etc.)
statementLines: The number of international airports in India is
questions.questionText: The number of international airports in India is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. In the **early 1980s, India had 5 international airports**: 1. **Mumbai (Bombay)** 2. **Delhi** 3. **Chennai (Madras)** 4. **Kolkata (Calcutta)** 5. **Thiruvananthapuram (Trivandrum)** These were the **primary international gateways** for trade, travel, and connectivity at the time.
Since then, India has **expanded to 26 international airports** (e.g., Hyderabad, Bengaluru, Cochin, Goa). Hence,

option b is correct

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: The number of French Mirage-2000 fighter aircraft that France has agreed to supply to India between 1984"86 is
questions.questionText: The number of French Mirage-2000 fighter aircraft that France has agreed to supply to India between 1984"86 is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. In the early 1980s, India signed an agreement with France to **purchase 30 Mirage -**2000**fighter aircraft**, to be delivered between **1984** and **1986**.
Purpose: o **Strengthen the Indian Air Force** post
- **1971** war.
o **Balance regional power**, especially after **Pakistans acquisition of F-16s**. **Mirage -**2000**became one of Indias frontline multi-role fighters**, known for its agility and advanced avionics.
Hence,

option a is correct

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
statementLines: The death rate according to 1981 census is ____ per thousand persons
questions.questionText: The death rate according to 1981 census is ____ per thousand persons
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. According to the **1981 Census**, the **death rate in India was around 14.8** per 1000 persons annually.
Over the decades, **death rates in India have declined steadily** due to: o Improvements in **public health**. o **Expanded immunisation and medical access**.
o **Better sanitation and nutrition**. For comparison: By **2011, Indias death rate had dropped to around 7.4 per 1000**. Hence,

option a is correct

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
statementLines: The correct sequence of the following densely populated States is
1. Uttar Pradesh
2. Kerala
3. Bihar
4. West Bengal
Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.questionText: The correct sequence of the following densely populated States is 1. Uttar Pradesh 2. Kerala 3. Bihar 4. West Bengal Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. In the early 1980s, the **population density ranking** among the given states was: 1. **West Bengal** " **highest density** (over 600 persons/sq. km). 2. **Uttar Pradesh** " **second highest**, due to large population and moderate area.
3. **Kerala** " **third**, high density due to small area and significant population. 4. **Bihar** " **fourth** among these options at that time (later overtook UP in density).
Hence,

option b is correct

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Global Groupings and Agreements (FTA, Paris Agreement, NPT, QUAD, etc.)
statementLines: Melbourne declaration was connected with
questions.questionText: Melbourne declaration was connected with

options.c: Reducing the economic inequality between the developed and the developing countries

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. The **Melbourne Declaration (**1981**)** was adopted at the **Commonwealth Heads of Government Meeting (CHOGM)** in Melbourne.
It reaffirmed the Commonwealths commitment to a **fairer economic deal for developing countries**. It focused on **reducing global economic inequality** through **North"South cooperation**.
Emphasis was on **restructuring international trade, aid, finance, and technology transfer**. The declaration aligned with demands for a **New International Economic Order** advocated by developing countries.
It was not about energy or human rights but about **economic justice between developed and developing worlds**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Nehru Model of Development laid greater stress on
questions.questionText: Nehru Model of Development laid greater stress on

options.d: Rapid industrialisation with particular emphasis on the development of basic and heavy industries

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. The **Nehru"Mahalanobis model**, adopted from the **Second Five-Year Plan (**1956**"61)** onwards, stressed **rapid industrialisation**.
It focused on **basic and heavy industries (steel, power, machine tools)** as the **commanding heights** of the economy. The strategy was based on the belief that **capital goods industries would generate self-reliant growth**.
**Public sector was assigned a dominant role** in strategic industries. **Agriculture received subsidiary attention**, with **industry-led growth** being the main strategy.
This laid the foundation for **Indias planned industrial base** post-independence. Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Capital intensive industries
questions.questionText: Capital intensive industries

options.c: Give blow to the growth of large scale industries
options.d: Provide opportunities for workers participation in management

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Capital intensive industries** rely more on **machines and technology** than labour. They are typically **large-scale industries** like **steel, petrochemicals, power**, and **automobiles**. Their growth may **divert investment and resources**, affecting the **expansion of labour-intensive sectors**.
They can **reduce employment opportunities**, but **increase productivity**. The option (c) reflects that they **impact the development pattern**, influencing **large-scale industrial structure**.
Other options either relate to rural employment or labour participation, which is not their primary characteristic. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Main source of Indias national income is from
questions.questionText: Main source of Indias national income is from
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. By the early 1980s, **services sector had become the largest contributor** to Indias national income.
**Agriculture**, once dominant, saw its **share decline steadily** since the 1950s. **Industry also grew** but remained second to services in contribution.
**Transport, trade, communications, finance, and public administration** were key service contributors. This structural change reflects **economic diversification and urbanisation trends**.
Since none of the three options matches this, the correct answer is **none of these.** Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Wealth tax on agricultural property is levied by
questions.questionText: Wealth tax on agricultural property is levied by

options.c: Both the Central and State Governments

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Agricultural taxation is a state subject** under the Constitution. **Wealth tax on agricultural land can only be imposed by state governments**, not the Centre.
The **Central Wealth Tax Act excluded agricultural land** from its ambit, respecting the constitutional division of powers. States may impose taxes on **agricultural income, land, and property**, though in practice such taxes are often minimal. This reflects the federal structure where **Centre handles non-agricultural taxation and States handle agricultural**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Multinational means
questions.questionText: Multinational means

options.a: A person having passports and visas of many countries
options.b: A person having property in many countries
options.c: A person holding citizenship of many countries
options.d: A company operating in more than one country

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. A **multinational corporation (MNC)** is an enterprise **operating in two or more countries**.
It **owns or controls production, marketing, or service facilities abroad**, beyond its home country. Examples include **Unilever, IBM, Toyota**, etc.
MNCs bring **capital, technology, and managerial know-how**, but also may influence domestic policies. Their operations are often regulated by **Bilateral Investment Treaties (BITs)** and national laws. They are key players in **globalisation and international trade flows**.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
questions.microTopic: Allied Sectors (Animal Husbandry, Horticulture, Sericulture, Apiculture, Aquaculture)
statementLines: Operation Flood refers to
questions.questionText: Operation Flood refers to

options.a: Use of preventive measures for flood devastation
options.c: Dairy development for increased milk production

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Operation Flood**, launched in **1970**, was a project of the **National Dairy Development Board (NDDB)**.
It was the **worlds largest dairy development programme**, aimed at creating a **White Revolution** in India. It linked **rural milk producers with urban markets** through a **National Milk Grid**, ensuring **stable prices and remunerative returns**.
It was implemented in three phases (**1970**"**1996**), leading India to become the **largest milk producer in the world**. The model was based on **village cooperatives, empowering rural farmers**, increasing incomes, and ensuring regular supply.
It transformed India from a **milk-deficient country to a milk-surplus nation**. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which tax is not levied by the Central Government?
questions.questionText: Which tax is not levied by the Central Government?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Profession tax is levied by state governments** and local bodies under **Article 276** of the Constitution.
It applies to individuals engaged in **professions, trades, callings, and employment**. The power to levy **excise duty, income tax (non-agricultural)**, and **wealth tax rests with the Union Government**.
**Wealth tax (abolished in**2015**)** was a Central levy on net wealth above a threshold. **Profession tax is usually a small, fixed amount** collected by state governments as part of their own revenue sources.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
statementLines: Which of the following States in India has the highest density of population?
questions.questionText: Which of the following States in India has the highest density of population?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. As per the **2011 Census**, **Bihar** has the **highest population density** among Indian states.
Bihars population density: **1102 persons per sq. km**, surpassing West Bengal (1029), Kerala (859), Uttar Pradesh (828). **High density is due to high fertility rates, agrarian rural settlements**, and limited out-migration relative to population growth.
The **Gangetic plains fertile soil supports intensive agriculture and dense habitation**. **Infrastructure and resources face heavy pressure** due to this density.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: States earn more revenue directly through which of the following taxes?
questions.questionText: States earn more revenue directly through which of the following taxes?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Before GST**, **sales tax** (now subsumed into SGST) was the **largest source of tax revenue for states**.
It was a **state subject**, levied on **intra-state sale of goods**, collected by retailers and remitted to state governments. **Customs duties are a Union subject**, collected by the Centre at ports. **Excise duties (Central Excise)** were also levied by the Union on manufacturing.
**Income tax (except agricultural income)** is levied by the Centre, though states receive a share through devolution. **Sales tax formed a substantial proportion** of states own tax revenue, financing their expenditure.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: First Five-Year Plan laid stress on
questions.questionText: First Five-Year Plan laid stress on
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **First Five-Year Plan (**1951**"56)** focused on **agriculture and irrigation** to achieve **food security** in the post-Independence period.
The economy faced **acute food shortages, refugee rehabilitation issues**, and **foreign exchange constraints** after Partition. Major emphasis was on **community development, land reforms, irrigation projects** like Bhakra-Nangal, and **agricultural extension services**.
**Industry received secondary priority**, mainly focusing on consumer goods and rehabilitation of existing industries. It targeted a **growth rate of 2.1%**, and achieved **3.6%**, largely due to **good harvests**.
The strategy was based on **Harrod"Domar growth model**, stressing **capital accumulation through agriculture**. Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The rupee exchange rate is currently linked with
questions.questionText: The rupee exchange rate is currently linked with
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **By early 1980s**, India shifted from a sterling peg to a **basket-peg system** to **stabilise the rupee**.
The **exchange rate was linked to a basket of currencies** of major trading partners. This **diversified currency risk** compared to pegging with a single currency.
The system allowed more **realistic exchange rate movements** in line with trade patterns. It was a **managed floating regime**, precursor to later liberalised exchange systems.
Hence, option (d) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Mixed economy means
questions.questionText: Mixed economy means

options.a: Coexistence of small and large scale industries
options.b: Coexistence of private and public sectors
options.c: Promotion of agriculture as well as industries

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. A **mixed economy** involves **both public and private sectors** working together. India adopted this model post-independence " **strategic industries under public sector**, consumer & small industries under private.
It aims to combine **social welfare with market efficiency**. The **Planning Commission** directed resource allocation in key sectors. This structure dominated Indian economy **until liberalisation in 1991**. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Polity
statementLines: White paper is a
questions.questionText: White paper is a

options.c: Document published by the government on matters of national importance

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. A **White Paper** is an **authoritative government document** on **policy issues of national importance**.
It explains **problems, policy positions, or proposed legislation**. Used to **inform the public and Parliament**, and often precedes reforms.
Examples include **white papers on economic reforms, telecom policy**, etc. It is not about physical paper but a **policy communication instrument**.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: Irrigation facilities should be improved urgently in India because
questions.questionText: Irrigation facilities should be improved urgently in India because

options.c: Rivers are dry most of the period in the year
options.d: Land under irrigation is very small

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Indian agriculture is **monsoon-dependent**, which is **seasonal and uncertain**. **Erratic monsoon patterns** lead to droughts or floods, impacting **farm productivity**. Improving **irrigation infrastructure** ensures **stable water supply and reduces vulnerability**.
Better irrigation leads to **higher cropping intensity** and **productivity gains**. **Monsoon irregularity** is the most urgent reason for irrigation development, especially for **food security**.
Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: The largest source of energy in India is
questions.questionText: The largest source of energy in India is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Thermal power contributes the largest share** in Indias total installed capacity. It primarily **relies on coal** (about 70"72% of domestic coal is used for power generation). Thermal plants provide **base-load electricity**, essential for industry and urban use.
**Hydel and nuclear capacities are smaller**; solar was minimal in **1982**. Indias energy mix has historically been **coal-dominated** due to **availability and cost factors**.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Global Groupings and Agreements (FTA, Paris Agreement, NPT, QUAD, etc.)
statementLines: North-South dialogue has reference to
questions.questionText: North-South dialogue has reference to

options.c: North and South countries of the world

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **North"South Dialogue** refers to negotiations between **developed (North)** and **developing (South) countries** during the 1970s"80s.
It focused on reforming **international economic relations** to make them more **equitable**. Developing countries demanded a **New International Economic Order (NIEO)** for fairer trade and finance systems.
The dialogue took place in forums like **UNCTAD and the UN General Assembly**. It reflected growing **assertiveness of Third World countries** in global economic governance.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: Which of the following shipyards makes warships?
questions.questionText: Which of the following shipyards makes warships?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd., Mumbai** is Indias **premier warship building yard**. It manufactures **warships and submarines for the Indian Navy**, along with offshore platforms. It has built major naval vessels like **destroyers, frigates, and submarines**.
It plays a **strategic role in Indias defence production and indigenisation**. Other shipyards like Cochin build commercial vessels but **Mazagon specializes in combat ships**.
Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: NABARD, which is set up to provide loans for rural development, is a
questions.questionText: NABARD, which is set up to provide loans for rural development, is a
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **NABARD** stands for **National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development**, set up in **1982**.
It functions as an **apex development bank** for financing **agriculture and rural sectors**. It was established with an **initial capital of 100 crore**, later increased to 5000 crore.
It focuses on **policy, planning and operations** in rural credit, refinancing **cooperative banks and RRBs**. It plays a crucial role in **rural infrastructure development** and **priority sector lending**.
Hence, option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following was not a factor for retardation of industrial growth last year?
questions.questionText: Which of the following was not a factor for retardation of industrial growth last year?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **Industrial growth slowdown** in late 1970s and early 1980s was due to **energy shortages, raw material bottlenecks, and financial constraints**, not **labour unrest**.
Wars of **1965** & **1971**, back-to-back droughts, and **licence raj complexities** were major structural causes of industrial deceleration.
**Low farm growth** restricted raw material supply to industries. **Inefficient infrastructure and control mechanisms** aggravated the problem.
**Labour disturbances were not a significant factor** during this slowdown phase. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The recent IMF loan to India will help immediately
questions.questionText: The recent IMF loan to India will help immediately

options.a: To meet the expenditure of 20-point programme
options.b: To meet the expenditure of Sixth Five-Year Plan
options.c: To correct the adverse balance of payments

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **IMF loans** are generally extended to support countries facing **BoP (Balance of Payments) crises**.
In **1981**"82, India borrowed **SDR 3.9 billion** under the **Extended Fund Facility**, the largest arrangement in IMF history at the time. The main purpose was to rectify **Current Account Deficit** and **stabilize foreign exchange reserves**. Such loans are **not meant for plan expenditure or poverty eradication directly**, but to meet **short-term external payment obligations**.
**IMF assistance improves foreign exchange liquidity** and stabilizes the rupee in crisis years. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Which of the following is incorrect?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is incorrect?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. Correct timeline of Plans: o **First Plan (**1951**"56)** " agriculture, irrigation focus. o **Second Plan (**1956**"61)** " Mahalanobis model, rapid industrialisation. o **Third Plan (**1961**"66)** " self-reliance, mixed results (wars, droughts).
o **Plan holiday (**1966**"69)** " three annual plans. o **Fourth Plan (**1969**"74)** " growth with stability. So, the **Fourth Plan was**1969**"74**, not **1966**"71.
Hence,

option d is correct

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: What is the main objective of the Rural Development Programme?
questions.questionText: What is the main objective of the Rural Development Programme?

options.a: To provide roads in the rural areas
options.b: To meet the growing and complex needs of rural people
options.c: To assist the poorest among the poor in rural areas in employment
options.d: To bring about overall development of rural India covering resources and cultural and economic aspects of the community life

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Rural development programmes** aim at **holistic development of rural areas** " economic, social, cultural and infrastructural.
Objectives include: o **Poverty alleviation** and **employment generation**. o Building **basic infrastructure** (roads, schools, irrigation, health).
o Improving **agriculture productivity, education, healthcare**, and **livelihoods**. o Encouraging **local participation and resource mobilisation**.
Employment and infrastructure are components, but the **core aim is overall integrated rural development**. Hence,

option d is correct

questions.subject: Science & Technology
statementLines: How many nuclear power plants are there in India now?
questions.questionText: How many nuclear power plants are there in India now?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. By the 2010s, India had **21 operational nuclear reactors** in **7 power stations**. These include stations like **Tarapur, Rawatbhata, Kalpakkam, Kakrapar, Kaiga, Kudankulam** etc. Installed capacity **5,780 MW**, generating around **30,000+ GWh annually**.
**6 more reactors** were under construction adding another **4,300 MW**. This reflects Indias gradual but steady growth in the **nuclear power sector**, under both **domestic programmes and international collaboration**.
Hence,

option d is correct

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: The average rate of increase of per capita income in 1978"79 was
questions.questionText: The average rate of increase of per capita income in 1978"79 was
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. Indias **per capita income** rose at an average of about **4.7%** in **1978**"79, higher than earlier years.
Growth driven by: o **Recovery in agriculture** after monsoon fluctuations. o Improvement in **industrial output and exports**. o **Moderate population growth** compared to income growth. **Per capita income growth** is a key indicator of **standard of living**, as it adjusts for population growth unlike GDP growth.
Hence,

option b is correct

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: What is land ceiling?
questions.questionText: What is land ceiling?

options.a: To fix a land area for irrigation purposes
options.b: To fix rural land holdings at the existing level
options.c: To fix urban land holdings at the existing level
options.d: To fix the quantum of land held by a family

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Land ceiling** refers to the **legal limit** on the amount of land that an **individual or family can own**.
Purpose: o Prevent **concentration of land** in a few hands. o Promote **equitable distribution** and **agrarian reforms**. o Allow **redistribution of surplus land** to **landless and marginal farmers**. **Land ceiling acts** were passed by states from 1960s onward, with national guidelines in **1972** setting uniform limits (10"54 acres depending on land type).
It applies to both urban and rural areas, but the **original concept was agrarian**. Hence,

option d is correct

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: International Organizations (UN, IMF, World Bank, ASEAN, BRICS, etc.)
statementLines: What is the main purpose of the International Development Association?
questions.questionText: What is the main purpose of the International Development Association?

options.a: To make loans on more flexible terms for important development projects in underdeveloped countries
options.b: To make loans on more flexible terms for important development projects in developed countries
options.c: To participate in development of irrigation projects in developing countries

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **International Development Association (IDA)** is part of the **World Bank Group**, established in **1960**.
It provides **concessional loans and grants** to the **poorest developing countries** with: o **Low or zero interest rates**. o **Long repayment periods**. o Focus on **infrastructure, poverty alleviation**, and **development projects**. It complements the **International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (IBRD)**, which lends to **middle-income countries**.
**Developed countries dont receive IDA funds**. Hence,

option a is correct

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: What is the trade policy of India?
questions.questionText: What is the trade policy of India?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Indias **post-independence trade policy** had two main pillars: o **Import substitution** " encourage **domestic industries** by restricting imports through **tariffs, quotas, licensing**, and promote **self-reliance**.
o **Export promotion** " earn **foreign exchange**, **diversify markets**, and integrate India into **global trade**. This **dual approach** protected **infant industries** while incentivising **export growth**, particularly in the 1970s"80s.
Policy was neither purely export-oriented nor import-oriented. Hence,

option a is correct

questions.subject: Polity
questions.sectionGroup: Part XI: Relations between the Union and the States
statementLines: Grants-in-aid is given to the States for
questions.questionText: Grants-in-aid is given to the States for

options.c: Improving the development of backward areas
options.d: Various development schemes and rehabilitation purposes

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Grants-in-aid** are funds given by the **Central Government** to **State Governments** under **Article 275** of the Constitution.
Primary objective: **reduce regional imbalances** by assisting economically weaker or **backward states**. These grants help ensure **uniform development**, **financial stability**, and support **centrally sponsored schemes**.
**Finance Commission** recommends such transfers based on **need, backwardness, and revenue capacity**. While they do support schemes, the **main constitutional purpose** is bridging **inter-state disparities**.
Hence,

option b is correct

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Why the main oil refineries in India are situated near seaports?
questions.questionText: Why the main oil refineries in India are situated near seaports?

options.a: Because India needs much crude oil which is mostly imported
options.b: Because most of the crude oil is exported to other countries
options.c: Because sea water is required for refining crude oil
options.d: Because petrol is available near sea ports

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. India **imports a large portion** of its crude oil to meet **domestic demand**. Locating refineries near ports: o **Reduces transportation costs** of imported crude. o Allows for **easy unloading and processing** at refineries.
o Ensures **quick supply of petroleum products** inland. **Sea water is not essential** for refining, and India doesnt export crude on a large scale.
Major refineries: **Jamnagar, Vizag, Chennai, Mumbai** " all coastal. Hence,

option a is correct

questions.subject: Agriculture
statementLines: What is the best way of increasing agricultural output in India?
questions.questionText: What is the best way of increasing agricultural output in India?

options.c: By increasing the area under cultivation
options.d: By using better seeds, requisite amount of fertilisers and modern technology

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. Indias **agricultural productivity** cant increase substantially just by expanding area or using one input like water/fertilisers.
The **Green Revolution** showed that the most effective method is a **package approach**: o Use of **high-yielding variety (HYV) seeds**. o Balanced **fertiliser application** and **irrigation**. o Adoption of **modern technology** like mechanisation, pest control, and extension services.
**Expansion of cultivated area** has natural limits and water/fertiliser alone wont give sustained gains without **good seeds**. Hence,

option d is correct

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Largest nationalised Bank in India is
questions.questionText: Largest nationalised Bank in India is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option c is the correct answer. **State Bank of India (SBI)**, established in **1955**, is Indias **largest nationalised commercial bank**[cite: 216, 217].
Key facts: o **Majority government-owned.** o Has the **largest asset base, branch network**, and **customer base** among Indian banks[cite: 218].
o Plays a major role in **government borrowing, development projects**, and **financial inclusion**[cite: 219]. **RBI is not a commercial bank** but the **central bank**[cite: 220].
SBIs size dwarfs other nationalised banks like Central Bank or Bank of India[cite: 221]. Hence,

option c is correct

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: India and Neighbourhood (Pakistan, China, Nepal, Sri Lanka, etc.)
statementLines: Farakka agreement between Bangladesh and India implies
questions.questionText: Farakka agreement between Bangladesh and India implies

options.a: Sharing of river water of Ganges

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. The **Farakka Agreement** (signed **1977**; later renewed **1996**) is a **bilateral treaty** between **India and Bangladesh** regarding the **sharing of Ganga river waters** at the **Farakka Barrage**[cite: 213].
Objective: Ensure **fair distribution of Ganga waters**, particularly during the **dry season**, to meet **Bangladeshs downstream needs** and **Indias navigation and irrigation needs**[cite: 214].
It represents **cooperative water management**, not border or military arrangements[cite: 215]. Free navigation is a separate issue under bilateral protocols.
Hence,

option a is correct

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: The Bhilai Steel Plant was set up with the collaboration of the U.S.S.R., the Rourkela Steel Plant with the collaboration of West Germany, the Durgapur Steel Plant was constructed with the collaboration of
questions.questionText: The Bhilai Steel Plant was set up with the collaboration of the U.S.S.R., the Rourkela Steel Plant with the collaboration of West Germany, the Durgapur Steel Plant was constructed with the collaboration of
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. Indias **three major integrated steel plants** in the 1950s were set up with **foreign collaboration** as part of **Nehrus industrialisation strategy**:
o **Bhilai " USSR** o **Rourkela " West Germany** o **Durgapur " Britain** (with technical and financial assistance) These collaborations were strategic " aimed at **developing core heavy industries** with **global support**[cite: 211].
**Durgapurs collaboration with Britain** reflected strong Indo"British economic ties post-independence[cite: 212]. Hence,

option a is correct

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: To ensure the efficiency, economic condition, etc. there are Control Boards for each major project except one for which a statutory corporation has been set up. That project is
questions.questionText: To ensure the efficiency, economic condition, etc. there are Control Boards for each major project except one for which a statutory corporation has been set up. That project is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. Most **major irrigation and hydroelectric projects** in India have **Control Boards** to oversee planning, execution, and maintenance[cite: 206].
**Ramganga Project**, however, is unique " instead of a control board, a **statutory corporation was not set up**, making it an exception to the usual practice[cite: 207].
**Ramganga Dam (Kalagarh Dam)** is located on the **Ramganga River** in **Uttarakhand**, within **Jim Corbett National Park**[cite: 208]. Farakka, Bhakra Nangal, and Damodar Valley projects had **dedicated control boards or authorities**[cite: 210]. Hence,

option d is correct

questions.subject: Economy
questions.microTopic: Money (Evolution, Types, Digital Currency, Crypto, etc.)
statementLines: Whose signature is found on a hundred rupee note?
questions.questionText: Whose signature is found on a hundred rupee note?

options.b: Governor of Reserve Bank of India

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **All banknotes (except Re. 1)** issued in India bear the **signature of the Governor of the Reserve Bank of India**[cite: 200].
This signifies that the **RBI is the monetary authority**, and the notes are issued under the **RBI Act, 1934**. The **Re. 1 **note** is an exception " it carries the **signature of the Finance Secretary** since its issued by the **Government of India**[cite: 201, 202].
The President and Prime Minister have no role in currency issuance[cite: 203]. The Governors signature represents the **guarantee of the Central Bank** to honour the notes value[cite: 204].
Hence,

option b is correct

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Transport (Roadways, Railways, Waterways, Ports, Airways, Pipelines, etc.)
statementLines: Qantas Airways belongs to
questions.questionText: Qantas Airways belongs to
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. **Qantas Airways** is the **flag carrier of Australia**, founded in **1920**. It stands for **Queensland and Northern Territory Aerial Services.** [cite: 196] Qantas is known as **Australias largest and oldest airline**, operating domestic and international flights[cite: 197].
It played a key role in **linking Australia with Asia, Europe, and America**[cite: 198]. Other options are unrelated " Qantas has always been Australian.
Hence,

option a is correct

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Demography, Population, Census (Literacy, Sex Ratio, etc.)
statementLines: The only State in India that shows excess of females over males is
questions.questionText: The only State in India that shows excess of females over males is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. **Kerala** has historically been the **only Indian state** with a **sex ratio favouring females**[cite: 188, 189].
Reasons: o **High female literacy, better healthcare**, and **low female mortality**[cite: 190]. o **Progressive social indicators, matrilineal traditions** in some communities, and **higher life expectancy** among women[cite: 192].
Other states have traditionally had **male-dominated sex ratios** due to **socio-economic factors** and **skewed gender norms**[cite: 193]. Keralas demographic trend continues even today with a **sex ratio above 1000 females per 1000 males**[cite: 194]. Hence,

option b is correct

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Mixed economy refers to
questions.questionText: Mixed economy refers to

options.a: The coexistence of heavy, small scale and cottage industries
options.b: The promotion of agriculture as well as cottage industries
options.c: The coexistence of rich as well as poor
options.d: Co-existence of public as well as private sector

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. A **mixed economy** combines features of **capitalism and socialism**[cite: 182]. In India, this meant: o **Public sector dominance** in **core industries** (e.g., steel, banking, power)[cite: 183]. o **Private sector encouraged** in **agriculture, consumer goods, small industries**[cite: 184].
o **Regulation by the state**, but not full control " allowing **market forces to function** alongside planning[cite: 185]. This model was adopted post-Independence to achieve **growth with social justice**[cite: 186]. The other options describe sectoral or social combinations, not economic structure. Hence,

option d is correct

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Antyodaya scheme is meant
questions.questionText: Antyodaya scheme is meant

options.d: To help the economically weakest sections of the society

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Antyodaya Anna Yojana** or **Antyodaya scheme**, launched initially in Rajasthan (**1977**) and later adopted nationally (**1980**), targeted the **poorest of the poor**[cite: 178].
Aim: **Uplift the last person in the line** (Antyodaya = the rise of the last person)[cite: 179]. It focused on providing **economic assistance, employment, and essential services** to the **most economically deprived households**, irrespective of caste, community or religion[cite: 180].
Though Harijans and minorities often benefited, the **core criterion was economic weakness**[cite: 181]. Hence,

option d is correct

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Hong Kong is
questions.questionText: Hong Kong is
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. Hong Kong has traditionally been: o **A free port** " **no tariffs on imports**; only a few excise duties (e.g., on alcohol, tobacco, oil)[cite: 172, 173].
o **A free exchange market** " its economy is **highly open**, with **free flow of capital, goods, and services**[cite: 174]. o Though **not fully independent**, it has enjoyed **high economic and administrative autonomy** (especially pre
- **1997**) with its own **trade, financial and customs systems**[cite: 175].
Its currency is **linked to the US dollar**, and its known for being one of the **most open, competitive, and laissez-faire economies** in the world[cite: 176].
**All given options** describe features of Hong Kongs status, especially in the **1980** context[cite: 177]. Hence,

option d is correct

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Highest per capita income is in
questions.questionText: Highest per capita income is in
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option a is the correct answer. During the late 1970s, **Punjab** had the **highest per capita income** among the states listed[cite: 167].
Reasons: o **Green Revolution success** " high productivity in **wheat and rice**[cite: 168]. o **Rural prosperity**, better **irrigation, infrastructure**, and marketing systems.
o **Small population** relative to its **high agricultural output** led to **high per capita figures**[cite: 169]. Gujarat and Tamil Nadu were industrially strong but **Punjab led in per capita terms** then[cite: 170].
Hence,

option a is correct

questions.subject: Economy
statementLines: Rolling Plan can be best defined in context of India as
questions.questionText: Rolling Plan can be best defined in context of India as

options.b: Perspective of Five-Year Plan with the provision of extending by one year at a time so that there would be a constant planning horizon of five years
options.c: Aims and achievements reviewed every year in a Five-Year Plan

questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option b is the correct answer. The **Rolling Plan (**1978**"80)** was introduced by **Morarji Desais Janata Government**[cite: 162, 163].
**Key feature**: It had a **5-year perspective**, but every year the plan was **extended by one year**, hence maintaining a **rolling 5-year horizon continuously**[cite: 164].
It had three components: 1. **Annual Plan** for the current year. 2. **Fixed-term plan** (3"5 years) updated annually[cite: 165]. 3. **Long-term perspective plan** (10"15 years). This provided **flexibility**, unlike **rigid 5-year plans**[cite: 166].
Hence,

option b is correct

questions.subject: Geography
statementLines: Oil has been struck in which of the following places recently by the ONGC?
questions.questionText: Oil has been struck in which of the following places recently by the ONGC?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Explanation:** Option d is the correct answer. **Oil and Natural Gas Corporation (ONGC)** struck oil in the **Krishna"Godavari (KG) basin** in the late 1970s[cite: 158, 159].
